input.description: Read quickstart for canonical layout

content.content:
# Quickstart

This guide takes you from nothing to a synced workspace in a few minutes. It
covers authoring a **canonical repo**, wiring a workspace to it, and running
`sync` / `validate`.

A copyable example canonical repo lives in
[`examples/canonical/`](../examples/canonical/). The full, frozen format
contract is [`docs/spec/ir-v1.md`](spec/ir-v1.md) — this page stays at the
"what you need to get going" level.

---

## 1. Author a canonical repo

The canonical repo is a normal Git repository holding your shared agent
configuration. agent-sync reads a closed set of **six concept kinds**, each
living at a fixed path:

```
canonical-repo/
├── AGENTS.md                 # project guidance (agents-md)
├── rules/<id>.md             # one rule per file
├── skills/<id>/SKILL.md      # one skill per directory
├── commands/<id>.md          # one command per file
├── mcp/<server>.json         # one MCP server entry per file
└── plugins/<id>.toml         # one plugin reference per file
```

A node's **id comes from its filename or directory name** (`rules/foo.md` →
id `foo`; `skills/code-review/SKILL.md` → id `code-review`). Ids must match
`[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-_]{0,63}`.

### Frontmatter — the one thing that trips people up

Markdown-bearing files (`AGENTS.md`, rules, skills, commands) accept an
**optional** YAML frontmatter block. Only three fields are recognized:

```markdown
---
required: true          # fail the sync if a target can't support this node
targets: [claude, cursor]  # restrict to these adapters (omit = all targets)
version: 2              # author-controlled counter
---

The body goes here.
```

> **Gotcha:** Do **not** put native tool frontmatter like `name:` or
> `description:` in a canonical `SKILL.md`. The decoder rejects unknown
> frontmatter fields with a hard error. The skill's name comes from its
> directory; each adapter generates the tool-native frontmatter on emit.
> Unknown fields prefixed `x-` are tolerated (and ignored) for forward-compat.

---

## 2. Initialize a workspace

From the directory you want managed, point a workspace at your canonical repo.
Pinning to a commit SHA is the default (and required for a local path):

```bash
# Remote canonical repo (resolves the ref to a SHA for you):
agent-sync init --source https://github.com/your-org/agent-config --ref main \
  --target claude --target cursor --target codex

# Or a local canonical repo, pinned to a specific commit:
agent-sync init --local-path ../agent-config --commit <40-char-sha> \
  --target claude --target cursor

# Or per-repo skills authored right in this repo (no git, no pin):
agent-sync init --local-dir .agents --target claude --target codex
```

The `url`/`local_path` forms write a `.agent-sync.yaml` with a pinned `commit`
and `trusted_sha`. The `--local-dir` form points the workspace at an in-repo
directory (here `.agents`) read straight from the working tree: no commit, no
trust prompt, and it works offline — author skills under `.agents/skills/<id>/`,
rules under `.agents/rules/`, and so on, then sync. On a terminal, `agent-sync
init` with no flags runs an interactive wizard.

---

## 3. Sync

```bash
agent-sync sync
```

This materializes the pinned content, compiles it through each target's
adapter, and writes native files:

- **Reserved subdirectories** (agent-sync owns the whole directory):
  `.claude/rules/agent-sync/`, `.cursor/rules/agent-sync/`,
  `.claude/skills/agent-sync-<id>/`, `.codex/skills/agent-sync-<id>/`.
- **Tool-owned files** (agent-sync owns only a marked section/entry, your
  content is preserved): `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.mcp.json`,
  `.cursor/mcp.json`, `.codex/config.toml`.

In tool-owned files, agent-sync content lives between
`<!-- agent-sync:begin id=... -->` / `<!-- agent-sync:end id=... -->` markers
(markdown) or under `agentsync_<id>` keys (JSON/TOML). Edit anything outside
those — the next sync leaves it untouched.

---

## 4. Validate

`validate` is a read-only dry run — it reports drift without writing. It's the
right thing to run in a git hook or CI to answer "is this workspace in sync?":

```bash
agent-sync validate            # exit 0 = no drift, exit 1 = drift detected
agent-sync validate --output=json
```

Immediately after a clean `sync`, `validate` reports **no drift**. If you (or
a teammate) hand-edit inside an agent-sync-managed section, `validate` flags it
so the next `sync` re-rendering it is never a surprise.

content.text:
I have everything I need to design the reconciliation. Here's what the data shows and where the real decisions are.

## What "reconcile" actually involves

Three states are out of sync, and the remote is the *stale* one:

| Source | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| **`claude-config` remote (origin/main)** | Still holds the old `gstack/*` skills + `agents/` + `commands/`. **Stale.** |
| **Your local `~/.claude` working tree** | You've *deleted* all of that locally (4 commits behind + working-tree deletions of every gstack skill, both agents, the commands). |
| **What you actually run now** | Skills come from **plugins** — `compound-engineering`, `superpowers`, `kb`, `learning-opportunities`, `warp` — plus a few local skills (mostly personal: `cora-email`, `use-spark`, `plus-one-ssh`, `plane-time-tracking`; reusable: `react-nextjs`, `impeccable`). |

So reconciling = **replacing the dead gstack model with your current plugin-based reality**, re-expressed in agent-sync's canonical layout, and de-personalized for team use.

**The canonical layout** agent-sync reads (confirmed from source):
```
claude-config/
├── CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md     # agents-md  ← your engineering standards
├── rules/<id>.md             # focused rules (optional split of CLAUDE.md)
├── skills/<id>/SKILL.md      # authored team skills
├── commands/<id>.md
├── mcp/<server>.json         # one MCP server per file
└── plugins/<id>.toml         # plugin references (compound-engineering, superpowers, kb…)
```

## Three things that block a clean answer

**1. Secrets are currently in plaintext.** `settings.json` has your Langfuse `pk-`/`sk-` keys hardcoded in `env`, and the `~/.claude.json` MCP servers carry tokens. None of that can go to a team repo verbatim.

**2. Your MCP set is multi-client.** `actual-plane` is Actual Reality; but `ojtech-plane` and `appliedLabsPlane` are *other clients* and shouldn't ship team-wide. The rest (`playwright`, `context7`, `sequential-thinking`, `chrome-devtools`, `posthog`, `airtable`, `pencil`, `monologue`) are generic/personal-mix.

**3. Skills = plugins, not files.** Your real skill engines are marketplace plugins. agent-sync's `plugin-reference` kind is built exactly for this — far better than copying skill files the team would then have to maintain.

Let me lock the three judgment calls that change the output:

questions.question: How should secrets (Langfuse keys, MCP tokens) be handled in the team canonical repo?

options.label: Env-var placeholders
options.description: Canonical files reference ${LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY} etc.; each engineer sets real values locally (shell env / a gitignored file) outside agent-sync. Repo stays secret-free.

options.label: Omit secret-bearing config
options.description: Leave Langfuse hook + any token-bearing MCP servers OUT of the team repo entirely. Engineers add their own. Simplest, but loses shared observability/MCP defaults.

options.label: Secrets manager
options.description: Wire values from 1Password/Doppler/etc. via a wrapper. Most robust, most setup — likely overkill for v1.

questions.question: What's the team skill strategy in claude-config?

options.label: Plugin references only
options.description: plugins/*.toml referencing compound-engineering, superpowers, kb. No copied skill files. Team installs the same plugins; updates flow from upstream. Matches how you actually work now.

options.label: Plugins + a few team skills
options.description: Plugin references PLUS copy genuinely reusable authored skills (e.g. react-nextjs, impeccable) into skills/. More to maintain but self-contained.

options.label: Resurrect gstack
options.description: Bring back the old gstack skills from origin/main as canonical skills. (You deleted these locally — likely not what you want.)

questions.question: Which MCP servers belong in the Actual Reality team set?

options.label: Generic tools + actual-plane

content.text: All decisions locked. Let me pull the exact definitions for the 6 MCP servers I'm keeping so the canonical `mcp/*.json` files are real (with secret values replaced by `${ENV}` placeholders, not literals).

input.command:
cd ~ && python3 - <<'PY'
import json,re
d=json.load(open('.claude.json'))
keep=['playwright','context7','sequential-thinking','chrome-devtools','posthog','actual-plane']
srv=d.get('mcpServers',{})
for k in keep:
    v=srv.get(k)
    if not v:
        print(f"## {k}: NOT FOUND"); continue
    # sanitize env values -> placeholder by key name
    redacted=json.loads(json.dumps(v))
    env=redacted.get('env',{})
    for ek in list(env.keys()):
        env[ek]="${%s}"%ek
    # redact secrets embedded in args/url/headers
    def scrub(s):
        return re.sub(r'(eyJ|sk-|pk-|ghp_|github_pat_)[A-Za-z0-9._-]+','${SECRET}',s) if isinstance(s,str) else s
    if 'args' in redacted: redacted['args']=[scrub(a) for a in redacted['args']]
    if 'url' in redacted: redacted['url']=scrub(redacted['url'])
    if 'headers' in redacted: redacted['headers']={hk:scrub(hv) for hk,hv in redacted['headers'].items()}
    print(f"## {k}\n{json.dumps(redacted,indent=2)}\n")
PY

content.content:
1	package ir
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"io"
9		"sort"
10		"strings"
11	
12		"github.com/goccy/go-yaml"
13	)
14	
15	// Frontmatter is the recognized metadata block for a Node. The decoder
16	// extracts it from the source file and applies defaults for any field the
17	// author didn't set.
18	//
19	// Field defaults match the spec:
20	//   - Required: false
21	//   - Targets:  empty slice (means "all adapters")
22	//   - Version:  1
23	type Frontmatter struct {
24		Required bool     `yaml:"required" json:"__agentsync_required"`
25		Targets  []string `yaml:"targets"  json:"__agentsync_targets"`
26		Version  int      `yaml:"version"  json:"__agentsync_version"`
27	}
28	
29	// defaultFrontmatter returns a fresh Frontmatter with the documented
30	// defaults applied. Targets is an empty (non-nil) slice so the shape is
31	// stable regardless of source format (YAML/JSON/TOML); the spec contract
32	// is "empty == all adapters".
33	func defaultFrontmatter() Frontmatter {
34		return Frontmatter{
35			Required: false,
36			Targets:  []string{},
37			Version:  1,
38		}
39	}
40	
41	// applyDefaults fills in zero-value fields with documented defaults. Used
42	// after parsing a partial frontmatter block. Normalizes Targets to a
43	// non-nil empty slice so the shape is consistent across all extractors
44	// (a parsed YAML doc that omits `targets:` and a JSON file with no
45	// `__agentsync_targets` key both produce `[]string{}`, never nil).
46	func (fm *Frontmatter) applyDefaults() {
47		if fm.Version == 0 {
48			fm.Version = 1
49		}
50		if fm.Targets == nil {
51			fm.Targets = []string{}
52		}
53	}
54	
55	// extractMarkdownFrontmatter splits a markdown source into (frontmatter,
56	// body). When the source has no leading `---\n` block, returns
57	// defaultFrontmatter and the input unchanged.
58	//
59	// Frontmatter syntax: `---\n` on its own line, YAML, `---\n` on its own
60	// line. CR/LF tolerated. Unknown YAML fields are errors except the `x-`
61	// prefix (forward-compat).
62	func extractMarkdownFrontmatter(src []byte) (Frontmatter, []byte, error) {
63		const sep = "---"
64	
65		// Quick check: source must start with `---` followed by a newline.
66		if !startsWithDelimiter(src, sep) {
67			fm := defaultFrontmatter()
68			return fm, src, nil
69		}
70	
71		// Skip the opening delimiter line.
72		rest := skipFirstLine(src)
73	
74		// Find the closing `---` delimiter on its own line.
75		closeIdx := findDelimiterLine(rest, sep)
76		if closeIdx < 0 {
77			return Frontmatter{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("%w: missing closing --- delimiter", ErrFrontmatterParse)
78		}
79	
80		yamlBytes := rest[:closeIdx]
81		body := skipFirstLine(rest[closeIdx:])
82	
83		fm, err := parseYAMLFrontmatter(yamlBytes)
84		if err != nil {
85			return Frontmatter{}, nil, err
86		}
87		fm.applyDefaults()
88		return fm, body, nil
89	}
90	
91	// extractJSONFrontmatter parses a JSON file, peels off any reserved
92	// `__agentsync_*` top-level keys, and returns a Frontmatter plus a re-emitted
93	// body with those keys stripped. Non-agent-sync keys preserve their original
94	// ordering and structure (encoding/json's map ordering is sorted, which
95	// is what JSON consumers should already tolerate).
96	//
97	// When the body fails to parse as JSON, returns ErrFrontmatterParse — the
98	// canonical-repo file isn't valid JSON, period.
99	func extractJSONFrontmatter(src []byte) (Frontmatter, []byte, error) {
100		if len(bytes.TrimSpace(src)) == 0 {
101			return Frontmatter{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("%w: empty JSON body", ErrFrontmatterParse)
102		}
103	
104		// Decode as a generic map so we can pick out reserved keys without
105		// imposing a schema on the rest of the document.
106		var raw map[string]json.RawMessage
107		dec := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader(src))
108		dec.UseNumber()
109		if err := dec.Decode(&raw); err != nil {
110			return Frontmatter{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("%w: %w", ErrFrontmatterParse, err)
111		}
112		// Reject trailing data after the first value. The JSON decoder will
113		// happily stop at the end of the first object, so `{"a":1}{"b":2}` or
114		// `{"a":1} trailing` would silently lose everything past the first
115		// brace. For mcp/*.json, that masks merge-corrupted files; require
116		// EOF (modulo whitespace) to ensure end-to-end validity.
117		var probe json.RawMessage
118		if err := dec.Decode(&probe); !errors.Is(err, io.EOF) {
119			if err == nil {
120				return Frontmatter{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("%w: trailing data after JSON object", ErrFrontmatterParse)
121			}
122			return Frontmatter{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("%w: trailing data after JSON object: %w", ErrFrontmatterParse, err)
123		}
124	
125		fm := defaultFrontmatter()
126	
127		// Reserved keys are best-effort: if the field exists but doesn't
128		// unmarshal cleanly into its target type, return ErrFrontmatterParse.
129		if v, ok := raw["__agentsync_required"]; ok {
130			if err := json.Unmarshal(v, &fm.Required); err != nil {
131				return Frontmatter{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("%w: __agentsync_required must be bool", ErrFrontmatterParse)
132			}
133			delete(raw, "__agentsync_required")
134		}
135		if v, ok := raw["__agentsync_targets"]; ok {
136			if err := json.Unmarshal(v, &fm.Targets); err != nil {
137				return Frontmatter{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("%w: __agentsync_targets must be []string", ErrFrontmatterParse)
138			}
139			delete(raw, "__agentsync_targets")
140		}
141		if v, ok := raw["__agentsync_version"]; ok {
142			if err := json.Unmarshal(v, &fm.Version); err != nil {
143				return Frontmatter{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("%w: __agentsync_version must be int", ErrFrontmatterParse)
144			}
145			delete(raw, "__agentsync_version")
146		}
147		fm.applyDefaults()
148	
149		// Re-emit the stripped body. Use deterministic key ordering so the
150		// output is byte-stable across decode runs (a determinism invariant).
151		body, err := marshalJSONStable(raw)
152		if err != nil {
153			return Frontmatter{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("%w: re-emit failed: %w", ErrFrontmatterParse, err)
154		}
155		return fm, body, nil
156	}
157	
158	// extractTOMLFrontmatter is a v1 stub: it returns default frontmatter and
159	// the body unchanged. Reserved-key extraction in TOML is deferred per
160	// docs/spec/ir-v1.md "Frontmatter on non-markdown nodes".

content.text:
I have the full picture now — and the source code surfaced one thing that changes your "plugin references only" decision, so I need to flag it before building.

## ⚠️ The one gotcha: Claude doesn't support `plugin-reference`

From `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/capabilities.go:46` — `plugin-reference` is **`Unsupported`** in the Claude adapter (also Cursor/Codex). It emits a warning and skips:

> *"Claude Code does not load project-level plugin-reference registries; install plugins via Claude Code's own plugin command"*

Your plugins work today because they're wired in `~/.claude/settings.json` (`enabledPlugins` + `extraKnownMarketplaces`) — a mechanism **agent-sync does not manage**. So "plugin references only" through agent-sync would deliver *zero skills* to the team. The fix is a **two-part setup**, which is normal for this:

1. **agent-sync canonical** (`claude-config`) → manages CLAUDE.md standards + MCP servers (the parts it *can* render).
2. **Plugin bootstrap** → a committed `settings.json` fragment + `scripts/setup.sh` in the same repo that installs the marketplaces/plugins. Outside agent-sync's render, but lives in the same repo so it's one clone.

I'll still record the plugin set as `plugins/*.toml` (honest team-standard declaration; future-proof for Pi/Codex), but the *real* install path is the settings fragment.

## Proposed reconciled `claude-config`

```
claude-config/
├── CLAUDE.md                       # agents-md ← de-personalized eng standards
├── README.md                       # rewritten: agent-sync model, drop deprecation note
├── mcp/
│   ├── playwright.json   context7.json   sequential-thinking.json
│   ├── chrome-devtools.json   posthog.json   actual-plane.json   # PLANE_* via ${env}
├── plugins/                        # team-standard plugin declarations (intent)
│   ├── compound-engineering.toml   superpowers.toml   kb.toml
├── settings.team.json              # enabledPlugins + extraKnownMarketplaces (the real install)
├── scripts/setup.sh                # installs plugins, points env vars
└── (removed) skills/gstack/**, agents/**, commands/**   # stale — you deleted these locally
```

**Engineer onboarding** becomes:
```bash
# user-level (~/.claude) managed by agent-sync:
agent-sync init --source https://github.com/Actual-Reality/claude-config --ref main --target claude
agent-sync sync --user        # renders CLAUDE.md + mcp into ~/.claude
bash <(curl .../scripts/setup.sh)   # installs the plugins agent-sync can't
# then set PLANE_API_KEY, LANGFUSE_* in your shell env
```

**Reconciliation decisions baked in:** stale gstack/agents/commands removed; MCP trimmed to generic + `actual-plane` (ojtech/appliedLabs/monologue/airtable/pencil dropped); all secrets → `${ENV}` placeholders; Langfuse hook shipped with placeholder keys, not yours.

---

Two things genuinely need your call before I author ~15 files in a team repo and open a PR:

input.content:
# Actual Reality — Agent Standards

Team-wide defaults for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex). This file
is the canonical `agents-md` node: `agent-sync` renders it into each tool's
native guidance file (a managed section in `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md`). Edit it
**here**, not in the rendered output.

This is a deliberately lean starter. Project-specific depth (detailed testing
philosophy, observability, language style guides) lives in each repo's own
`CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md`, outside the agent-sync-managed section.

## Stack

| Area | Stack |
|------|-------|
| Frontend | Bun + Next.js (App Router) + React 19 + TypeScript (strict) |
| Backend | Go 1.24+ + Echo v4 + oapi-codegen (strict) + pgx + sqlc + goose |
| Data / Python | Python 3.12+ + uv + FastAPI + Pydantic v2 + Ruff + mypy (strict) |
| DB | PostgreSQL (Neon or Railway) |

`uv` is mandatory for Python — no `pip`, `requirements.txt`, Poetry, or Conda.

## Architecture (non-negotiable)

Every request flows through layers; never skip one:

```
Handler/Route → Service → (Repository | Gateway) → I/O
```

| Layer | Owns | May import |
|-------|------|------------|
| Handler/Route | HTTP/UI parsing, auth, response shaping | Service interfaces |
| Service | Business logic, orchestration, transactions | Repo/Gateway interfaces, Domain |
| Repository | Internal persistence (our DB) | DB client, Domain |
| Gateway | External systems (APIs, SDKs, queues) | HTTP client, vendor SDKs, Domain |
| Domain | Types, value objects, sentinel errors | Standard library only |

- Services depend on **interfaces**, never concrete types; wire concretes at the
  composition root (`cmd/main.go`, route/page entry, or app factory).
- Services own transaction boundaries. Repositories accept a transaction handle.
- Gateways normalize vendor responses into Domain types — callers never see SDK types.
- Generated code (`*.gen.go`, sqlc, Drizzle introspection) is never hand-edited.
- Don't abstract before the third duplication.

## Verification (run before declaring done)

Every change passes its stack's gate. Fix root causes, not symptoms. If a test
fails, fix the code — not the test.

- **TS:** `bun run lint && bun run test`
- **Go:** `go vet ./... && go test -race ./... && golangci-lint run`
- **Python:** `uv run ruff check . && uv run mypy . && uv run pytest`

Verify with evidence, not assumptions. Never claim "done" or "tests pass"

- Atomic commits — one logical change each.
- Conventional commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `refactor:`, `test:`, `docs:`, `chore:`.
- Squash feature branches on merge.

toolUseResult.content:
# Actual Reality — Agent Standards

Team-wide defaults for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex). This file
is the canonical `agents-md` node: `agent-sync` renders it into each tool's
native guidance file (a managed section in `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md`). Edit it
**here**, not in the rendered output.

This is a deliberately lean starter. Project-specific depth (detailed testing
philosophy, observability, language style guides) lives in each repo's own
`CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md`, outside the agent-sync-managed section.

## Stack

| Area | Stack |
|------|-------|
| Frontend | Bun + Next.js (App Router) + React 19 + TypeScript (strict) |
| Backend | Go 1.24+ + Echo v4 + oapi-codegen (strict) + pgx + sqlc + goose |
| Data / Python | Python 3.12+ + uv + FastAPI + Pydantic v2 + Ruff + mypy (strict) |
| DB | PostgreSQL (Neon or Railway) |

`uv` is mandatory for Python — no `pip`, `requirements.txt`, Poetry, or Conda.

## Architecture (non-negotiable)

Every request flows through layers; never skip one:

```
Handler/Route → Service → (Repository | Gateway) → I/O
```

| Layer | Owns | May import |
|-------|------|------------|
| Handler/Route | HTTP/UI parsing, auth, response shaping | Service interfaces |
| Service | Business logic, orchestration, transactions | Repo/Gateway interfaces, Domain |
| Repository | Internal persistence (our DB) | DB client, Domain |
| Gateway | External systems (APIs, SDKs, queues) | HTTP client, vendor SDKs, Domain |
| Domain | Types, value objects, sentinel errors | Standard library only |

- Services depend on **interfaces**, never concrete types; wire concretes at the
  composition root (`cmd/main.go`, route/page entry, or app factory).
- Services own transaction boundaries. Repositories accept a transaction handle.
- Gateways normalize vendor responses into Domain types — callers never see SDK types.
- Generated code (`*.gen.go`, sqlc, Drizzle introspection) is never hand-edited.
- Don't abstract before the third duplication.

## Verification (run before declaring done)

Every change passes its stack's gate. Fix root causes, not symptoms. If a test
fails, fix the code — not the test.

- **TS:** `bun run lint && bun run test`
- **Go:** `go vet ./... && go test -race ./... && golangci-lint run`
- **Python:** `uv run ruff check . && uv run mypy . && uv run pytest`

Verify with evidence, not assumptions. Never claim "done" or "tests pass"
without execution output that supports it.

## Git

- Atomic commits — one logical change each.
- Conventional commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `refactor:`, `test:`, `docs:`, `chore:`.
- Squash feature branches on merge.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Bootstrap Actual Reality team plugins into ~/.claude/settings.json.
#
# agent-sync renders CLAUDE.md + MCP servers, but it cannot install Claude Code
# plugins (plugin-reference is an unsupported IR kind). This script handles that
# half: it deep-merges settings.team.json's `enabledPlugins` and
# `extraKnownMarketplaces` into your existing ~/.claude/settings.json, preserving
# everything else. Idempotent and backs up before writing.
set -euo pipefail

REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
TEAM_SETTINGS="$REPO_DIR/settings.team.json"
USER_SETTINGS="${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/settings.json"

[ -f "$TEAM_SETTINGS" ] || { echo "missing $TEAM_SETTINGS" >&2; exit 1; }
command -v python3 >/dev/null || { echo "python3 required" >&2; exit 1; }

mkdir -p "$(dirname "$USER_SETTINGS")"
[ -f "$USER_SETTINGS" ] || echo '{}' > "$USER_SETTINGS"
cp "$USER_SETTINGS" "$USER_SETTINGS.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

python3 - "$USER_SETTINGS" "$TEAM_SETTINGS" <<'PY'
import json, sys
user_path, team_path = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
user = json.load(open(user_path))
team = json.load(open(team_path))
for key in ("enabledPlugins", "extraKnownMarketplaces"):
    merged = {**user.get(key, {}), **team.get(key, {})}
    if merged:
        user[key] = merged
json.dump(user, open(user_path, "w"), indent=2)
print(f"merged team plugins into {user_path}")
PY

cat <<'EOF'

Plugins bootstrapped. Restart Claude Code so it installs the marketplaces.

Next, set the secrets these defaults expect (shell profile or a secrets tool):
  export PLANE_API_KEY=...          # Actual Reality Plane
  export PLANE_WORKSPACE_SLUG=...
  export PLANE_BASE_URL=...
  # Optional observability (if you enable the Langfuse Stop hook):
  export LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY=...
  export LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY=...
  export LANGFUSE_BASE_URL=https://us.cloud.langfuse.com

kb plugin is intentionally not installed yet — see plugins/kb.toml.
EOF

input.content:
# agent-config

Canonical AI-agent configuration for **Actual Reality**, consumed by
[`agent-sync`](https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync). One Git-backed
source of standards, MCP servers, and plugin defaults; `agent-sync` renders it
into each tool's native files (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex).

This replaces the old "clone this repo as `~/.claude`" model.

## Layout

```
CLAUDE.md            # team agent standards  → rendered as an agents-md node
mcp/<server>.json    # one MCP server per file (secrets via ${ENV} placeholders)
plugins/<id>.toml    # team-standard plugin declarations (intent; see note below)
settings.team.json   # plugin bootstrap merged into ~/.claude/settings.json
scripts/setup.sh     # installs the plugins agent-sync can't
```

`agent-sync` reads six concept kinds at fixed paths; see its
[quickstart](https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/blob/main/docs/quickstart.md).

## What syncs how

| Content | Mechanism | Notes |
|---------|-----------|-------|
| `CLAUDE.md` standards | `agent-sync sync` | Managed section in your tool's `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md`. |
| `mcp/*.json` | `agent-sync sync` | Written under `/mcpServers/agentsync_<id>` in `.mcp.json`. |
| Plugins | `scripts/setup.sh` | **Not** agent-sync — Claude Code doesn't load plugin-references. |
| Secrets | your shell env | `${PLANE_API_KEY}` etc. resolved per machine; never committed. |

## Onboarding

```bash
# 1. Plugins (one time, outside agent-sync):
git clone git@github.com:Actual-Reality/agent-config.git
bash agent-config/scripts/setup.sh

# 2. User-level standards + MCP via agent-sync (install agent-sync first):
agent-sync init --source https://github.com/Actual-Reality/agent-config \
  --ref main --target claude
agent-sync sync --user        # renders into ~/.claude

# 3. Set the secrets setup.sh printed (PLANE_*, optional LANGFUSE_*).
```

Per-project workspaces wire the same source with `agent-sync init` in the repo
root (drop `--user`, sync renders into the project's `.claude/` etc.).

## Conventions

- **No secrets in this repo.** Use `${ENV}` placeholders; resolve locally.
- MCP set is intentionally scoped to generic dev tools + `actual-plane`.
  Other-client Plane servers and personal MCPs are deliberately excluded.
- Edit standards in `CLAUDE.md` here — never in agent-sync-rendered output
  (it's overwritten on the next sync).

Done. Repo created, content built and committed locally, validated end-to-end, **not pushed**.

## What exists now

**`Actual-Reality/agent-config`** (private, created, empty on GitHub) — remote wired at `~/Projects/agent-config`, one commit ready on `main`:

```
CLAUDE.md            # minimal team standards (stack · layered arch · verify · git) → agents-md
mcp/                 # 6 servers, all secrets as ${ENV} placeholders
  playwright · context7 · sequential-thinking · chrome-devtools · posthog · actual-plane
plugins/             # team-standard declarations (intent)
  compound-engineering · superpowers · kb (kb = TODO: needs team-reachable source)
settings.team.json   # real plugin install (compound-engineering, superpowers)
scripts/setup.sh     # deep-merges plugin bootstrap into ~/.claude/settings.json
README.md · .gitignore
```

## Verified (not assumed)
- Canonical source **decodes cleanly** and `agent-sync sync` renders it: `.mcp.json` with all 6 servers, `${ENV}` placeholders preserved, `CLAUDE.md` managed section — **exit 0**.
- The three `plugin-reference` warnings fired exactly as predicted, confirming the two-part design (agent-sync for standards+MCP, `setup.sh` for plugins) is necessary and correct.

## Reconciliation decisions applied
- **Stale dropped** — gstack skills / team agents / timer commands don't come along (fresh repo).
- **MCP trimmed** to generic dev tools + `actual-plane`; ojtech/appliedLabs/monologue/airtable/pencil excluded.
- **Secrets** → `${ENV}` only; your real Langfuse/Plane keys never touched the repo.
- **CLAUDE.md** → minimal starter, de-personalized (no CE-routing/personal workflow).

## Open items for you
1. **kb plugin** — its marketplace is your local `~/Projects/kb` dir, unreachable team-wide. I left it declared with a TODO; give it a Git source and I'll wire it into `settings.team.json`.
2. **Push when ready** — `cd ~/Projects/agent-config && git push -u origin main`. Want me to push, or open it as a PR-style review first? (The repo's empty, so a direct push to `main` is the natural move.)

## Done
- Reconciled your three out-of-sync states into one fresh canonical source — stale gstack/agents/commands left behind, MCP trimmed to generic tools + `actual-plane`, secrets as `${ENV}` only, minimal de-personalized `CLAUDE.md`.
- Validated end-to-end before pushing: `agent-sync sync` renders it cleanly (exit 0).
- Per your call, **did not** wire your own `~/.claude` to it yet.

## What's left when you're ready
1. **kb plugin** — give it a team-reachable Git source (it's a local-dir marketplace today); then I'll add it to `settings.team.json` and `plugins/kb.toml`.
2. **Self-adopt** — `agent-sync init --source https://github.com/Actual-Reality/agent-config --ref main --target claude` then `agent-sync sync --user`, plus `bash scripts/setup.sh` for plugins. README has the full onboarding flow.
3. **Old `claude-config`** — still carries its deprecation notice; you may want to point its README at `agent-config` so the team lands in the right place.

One note for teammates: MCP tools resolve under the `mcp__agentsync_<server>__*` namespace once synced (agent-sync prefixes the keys), e.g. `mcp__agentsync_actual-plane__*`.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Bootstrap Actual Reality team plugins into ~/.claude/settings.json.
#
# agent-sync renders CLAUDE.md + MCP servers, but it cannot install Claude Code
# plugins (plugin-reference is an unsupported IR kind). This script handles that
# half: it deep-merges settings.team.json's `enabledPlugins` and
# `extraKnownMarketplaces` into your existing ~/.claude/settings.json, preserving
# everything else. Idempotent and backs up before writing.
set -euo pipefail

REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
TEAM_SETTINGS="$REPO_DIR/settings.team.json"
USER_SETTINGS="${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/settings.json"

[ -f "$TEAM_SETTINGS" ] || { echo "missing $TEAM_SETTINGS" >&2; exit 1; }
command -v python3 >/dev/null || { echo "python3 required" >&2; exit 1; }

mkdir -p "$(dirname "$USER_SETTINGS")"
[ -f "$USER_SETTINGS" ] || echo '{}' > "$USER_SETTINGS"
cp "$USER_SETTINGS" "$USER_SETTINGS.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

python3 - "$USER_SETTINGS" "$TEAM_SETTINGS" <<'PY'
import json, sys
user_path, team_path = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
user = json.load(open(user_path))
team = json.load(open(team_path))
for key in ("enabledPlugins", "extraKnownMarketplaces"):
    merged = {**user.get(key, {}), **team.get(key, {})}
    if merged:
        user[key] = merged
json.dump(user, open(user_path, "w"), indent=2)
print(f"merged team plugins into {user_path}")
PY

cat <<'EOF'

Plugins bootstrapped. Restart Claude Code so it installs the marketplaces.

Next, set the secrets these defaults expect (shell profile or a secrets tool):
  export PLANE_API_KEY=...          # Actual Reality Plane
  export PLANE_WORKSPACE_SLUG=...
  export PLANE_BASE_URL=...
  # Optional observability (if you enable the Langfuse Stop hook):
  export LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY=...
  export LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY=...
  export LANGFUSE_BASE_URL=https://us.cloud.langfuse.com

kb plugin is intentionally not installed yet — see plugins/kb.toml.
EOF

lastPrompt: How do we reconcile the scripts.sh and env vars, need an easy way to do that.

content.text:
Here's what the inspection shows — and it points to a clear design.

**Required vars (derived from the canonical files):** only `PLANE_API_KEY`, `PLANE_BASE_URL`, `PLANE_WORKSPACE_SLUG`. The Langfuse vars aren't referenced by any synced file yet (no hook shipped), so the static list `setup.sh` prints is already wrong on both ends — it lists Langfuse (not needed) and would miss any future server's vars. That drift is exactly what to fix.

**Your current state:**
- `PLANE_API_KEY` is exported **in plaintext in `~/.zshrc:227`** ⚠️ — and `PLANE_BASE_URL` / `PLANE_WORKSPACE_SLUG` aren't set at all, so your `actual-plane` MCP is relying on defaults today.
- `op` (1Password CLI) **is installed**; no Doppler.

**Resolution path:** Claude Code expands `${VAR}` in `.mcp.json` from the environment it's launched in — so whatever we do must end with those vars in your shell env when `claude` starts.

## The reconcile

Make `setup.sh` the single source of truth by **deriving** the required vars straight from `mcp/*.json` (scan for `${VAR}` tokens) instead of hardcoding a list. It then guarantees a gitignored env file holds exactly those keys and is sourced by your shell. Add a server with a new `${VAR}` → next `setup.sh` run picks it up automatically. No drift, ever.

The only real fork is **where the secret values live**, and since you've got `op`, that's a genuine choice:

questions.question: Where should the secret values live? (setup.sh derives the required keys from mcp/*.json either way — this is just how values are stored/resolved.)

options.label: 1Password (op) — recommended
options.description: Ship an env template with op:// references; setup.sh runs `op inject`/`op read` into a gitignored ~/.config/agent-config/env that your shell sources. Team shares a vault. Most secure, and you already have op. Also fixes the plaintext key in your .zshrc.

options.label: Plaintext gitignored env file
options.description: setup.sh writes ~/.config/agent-config/env, prompts for any missing values, sources it from .zshrc. Dead simple, no 1Password dependency, but secrets sit in plaintext on disk (same exposure as today, just centralized).

options.label: Storage-agnostic keys only
options.description: setup.sh only ensures the env file has the right KEYS (blank) + is sourced; each engineer fills values however they like (plaintext or `op read`). Most flexible, least hand-holding.

questions.question: Also migrate your existing plaintext PLANE_API_KEY out of ~/.zshrc into the new mechanism?

options.label: Yes, migrate it
options.description: Move the key out of .zshrc:227 into the chosen store and remove the plaintext export. Cleaner and safer.

options.label: Leave .zshrc alone
options.description: Don't touch your shell config; just set up the new mechanism alongside it.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Bootstrap Actual Reality team config on this machine. Two jobs:
#
#   1. Plugins  — deep-merge settings.team.json's `enabledPlugins` /
#      `extraKnownMarketplaces` into ~/.claude/settings.json. agent-sync can't
#      install Claude plugins (plugin-reference is an unsupported IR kind), so
#      this is the install mechanism.
#
#   2. Env vars — the MCP servers in mcp/*.json reference secrets as ${VAR}
#      placeholders, which Claude Code expands from its launch environment. This
#      script DERIVES the required vars straight from those files (single source
#      of truth, no drift), keeps them in a gitignored env file, sources that
#      file from your shell, and seeds any value already in your environment.
#
# Idempotent: re-run any time. Adds newly-introduced vars; never clobbers
# existing values.
set -euo pipefail

REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
TEAM_SETTINGS="$REPO_DIR/settings.team.json"
USER_SETTINGS="${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/settings.json"
ENV_FILE="${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/agent-config/env"

command -v python3 >/dev/null || { echo "python3 required" >&2; exit 1; }

# --- 1. Plugins -------------------------------------------------------------
if [ -f "$TEAM_SETTINGS" ]; then
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$USER_SETTINGS")"
  [ -f "$USER_SETTINGS" ] || echo '{}' > "$USER_SETTINGS"
  cp "$USER_SETTINGS" "$USER_SETTINGS.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  python3 - "$USER_SETTINGS" "$TEAM_SETTINGS" <<'PY'
import json, sys
user_path, team_path = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
user = json.load(open(user_path))
team = json.load(open(team_path))
for key in ("enabledPlugins", "extraKnownMarketplaces"):
    merged = {**user.get(key, {}), **team.get(key, {})}
    if merged:
        user[key] = merged
json.dump(user, open(user_path, "w"), indent=2)
print(f"plugins: merged team marketplaces into {user_path}")
PY
fi

# --- 2. Env vars ------------------------------------------------------------
# Derive required vars from the ${VAR} / ${VAR:-default} placeholders in mcp/*.json.
mapfile -t REQUIRED < <(
  grep -rhoE '\$\{[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*(:-[^}]*)?\}' "$REPO_DIR"/mcp/*.json 2>/dev/null \
    | sed -E 's/^\$\{([A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*).*/\1/' | sort -u
)

if [ "${#REQUIRED[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$ENV_FILE")"
  if [ ! -f "$ENV_FILE" ]; then
    umask 077
    printf '# Managed by agent-config/scripts/setup.sh — gitignored, local secrets.\n# Keys are derived from mcp/*.json. Re-run setup.sh to add new ones.\n' > "$ENV_FILE"
  fi
  chmod 600 "$ENV_FILE"

  for var in "${REQUIRED[@]}"; do
    if grep -qE "^export ${var}=" "$ENV_FILE"; then
      continue                                   # already provisioned — leave it
    fi
    val="${!var:-}"                              # seed from current environment if set
    if [ -z "$val" ] && [ -t 0 ]; then
      printf 'enter value for %s (blank to skip): ' "$var" >&2
      read -r val
    fi
    if [ -n "$val" ]; then
      printf 'export %s=%q\n' "$var" "$val" >> "$ENV_FILE"
      echo "env: set $var"
    else
      printf 'export %s=\n' "$var" >> "$ENV_FILE"
      echo "env: $var left blank — fill in $ENV_FILE" >&2
    fi
  done

  # Ensure the active shell rc sources the env file (idempotent).
  case "${SHELL##*/}" in
    zsh)  RC="$HOME/.zshrc" ;;
    bash) RC="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
    *)    RC="" ;;
  esac
  MARKER="# agent-config env"
  if [ -n "$RC" ] && ! grep -qF "$MARKER" "$RC" 2>/dev/null; then
    printf '\n%s\n[ -f "%s" ] && . "%s"\n' "$MARKER" "$ENV_FILE" "$ENV_FILE" >> "$RC"
    echo "env: added source line to $RC"
  fi

  # Warn about duplicate plaintext exports of managed vars in shell rc files.
  for rc in "$HOME/.zshrc" "$HOME/.zshenv" "$HOME/.zprofile" "$HOME/.bashrc" "$HOME/.bash_profile"; do
    [ -f "$rc" ] || continue
    for var in "${REQUIRED[@]}"; do
      if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*export ${var}=" "$rc"; then
        echo "warn: $var is also exported in $rc — now managed in $ENV_FILE; remove the duplicate" >&2
      fi
    done
  done
fi

cat <<EOF

Done. Restart your shell (or: source "$ENV_FILE") and restart Claude Code.
Required env vars live in: $ENV_FILE
EOF

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Bootstrap Actual Reality team config on this machine. Two jobs:
#
#   1. Plugins  — deep-merge settings.team.json's `enabledPlugins` /
#      `extraKnownMarketplaces` into ~/.claude/settings.json. agent-sync can't
#      install Claude plugins (plugin-reference is an unsupported IR kind), so
#      this is the install mechanism.
#
#   2. Env vars — the MCP servers in mcp/*.json reference secrets as ${VAR}
#      placeholders, which Claude Code expands from its launch environment. This
#      script DERIVES the required vars straight from those files (single source
#      of truth, no drift), keeps them in a gitignored env file, sources that
#      file from your shell, and seeds any value already in your environment.
#
# Idempotent: re-run any time. Adds newly-introduced vars; never clobbers
# existing values.
set -euo pipefail

REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
TEAM_SETTINGS="$REPO_DIR/settings.team.json"
USER_SETTINGS="${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/settings.json"
ENV_FILE="${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/agent-config/env"

command -v python3 >/dev/null || { echo "python3 required" >&2; exit 1; }

if [ -f "$TEAM_SETTINGS" ]; then
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$USER_SETTINGS")"

  cp "$USER_SETTINGS" "$USER_SETTINGS.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  python3 - "$USER_SETTINGS" "$TEAM_SETTINGS" <<'PY'
import json, sys
user_path, team_path = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
user = json.load(open(user_path))
team = json.load(open(team_path))
for key in ("enabledPlugins", "extraKnownMarketplaces"):
    merged = {**user.get(key, {}), **team.get(key, {})}
    if merged:
        user[key] = merged
json.dump(user, open(user_path, "w"), indent=2)
print(f"plugins: merged team marketplaces into {user_path}")
PY
fi

# --- 2. Env vars ------------------------------------------------------------

    | sed -E 's/^\$\{([A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*).*/\1/' | sort -u
)

if [ "${#REQUIRED[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$ENV_FILE")"
  if [ ! -f "$ENV_FILE" ]; then
    umask 077
    printf '# Managed by agent-config/scripts/setup.sh — gitignored, local secrets.\n# Keys are derived from mcp/*.json. Re-run setup.sh to add new ones.\n' > "$ENV_FILE"
  fi

    if grep -qE "^export ${var}=" "$ENV_FILE"; then
      continue                                   # already provisioned — leave it
    fi
    val="${!var:-}"                              # seed from current environment if set

      read -r val

    else

    bash) RC="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
    *)    RC="" ;;
  esac
  MARKER="# agent-config env"
  if [ -n "$RC" ] && ! grep -qF "$MARKER" "$RC" 2>/dev/null; then

fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Bootstrap Actual Reality team plugins into ~/.claude/settings.json.
#
# agent-sync renders CLAUDE.md + MCP servers, but it cannot install Claude Code
# plugins (plugin-reference is an unsupported IR kind). This script handles that
# half: it deep-merges settings.team.json's `enabledPlugins` and
# `extraKnownMarketplaces` into your existing ~/.claude/settings.json, preserving
# everything else. Idempotent and backs up before writing.
set -euo pipefail

REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
TEAM_SETTINGS="$REPO_DIR/settings.team.json"
USER_SETTINGS="${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/settings.json"

[ -f "$TEAM_SETTINGS" ] || { echo "missing $TEAM_SETTINGS" >&2; exit 1; }
command -v python3 >/dev/null || { echo "python3 required" >&2; exit 1; }

mkdir -p "$(dirname "$USER_SETTINGS")"
[ -f "$USER_SETTINGS" ] || echo '{}' > "$USER_SETTINGS"
cp "$USER_SETTINGS" "$USER_SETTINGS.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

python3 - "$USER_SETTINGS" "$TEAM_SETTINGS" <<'PY'
import json, sys
user_path, team_path = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
user = json.load(open(user_path))
team = json.load(open(team_path))
for key in ("enabledPlugins", "extraKnownMarketplaces"):
    merged = {**user.get(key, {}), **team.get(key, {})}
    if merged:
        user[key] = merged
json.dump(user, open(user_path, "w"), indent=2)
print(f"merged team plugins into {user_path}")
PY

cat <<'EOF'

Plugins bootstrapped. Restart Claude Code so it installs the marketplaces.

Next, set the secrets these defaults expect (shell profile or a secrets tool):
  export PLANE_API_KEY=...          # Actual Reality Plane
  export PLANE_WORKSPACE_SLUG=...
  export PLANE_BASE_URL=...
  # Optional observability (if you enable the Langfuse Stop hook):
  export LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY=...
  export LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY=...
  export LANGFUSE_BASE_URL=https://us.cloud.langfuse.com
EOF

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# agent-config

Canonical AI-agent configuration for **Actual Reality**, consumed by
[`agent-sync`](https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync). One Git-backed
source of standards, MCP servers, and plugin defaults; `agent-sync` renders it
into each tool's native files (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex).

This replaces the old "clone this repo as `~/.claude`" model.

## Layout

```
CLAUDE.md            # team agent standards  → rendered as an agents-md node
mcp/<server>.json    # one MCP server per file (secrets via ${ENV} placeholders)
plugins/<id>.toml    # team-standard plugin declarations (intent; see note below)
settings.team.json   # plugin bootstrap merged into ~/.claude/settings.json
scripts/setup.sh     # installs the plugins agent-sync can't
```

`agent-sync` reads six concept kinds at fixed paths; see its
[quickstart](https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/blob/main/docs/quickstart.md).

## What syncs how

| Content | Mechanism | Notes |
|---------|-----------|-------|
| `CLAUDE.md` standards | `agent-sync sync` | Managed section in your tool's `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md`. |
| `mcp/*.json` | `agent-sync sync` | Written under `/mcpServers/agentsync_<id>` in `.mcp.json`. |
| Plugins | `scripts/setup.sh` | **Not** agent-sync — Claude Code doesn't load plugin-references. |
| Secrets | your shell env | `${PLANE_API_KEY}` etc. resolved per machine; never committed. |

## Onboarding

```bash
# 1. Plugins (one time, outside agent-sync):
git clone git@github.com:Actual-Reality/agent-config.git
bash agent-config/scripts/setup.sh

# 2. User-level standards + MCP via agent-sync (install agent-sync first):
agent-sync init --source https://github.com/Actual-Reality/agent-config \
  --ref main --target claude
agent-sync sync --user        # renders into ~/.claude

# 3. Set the secrets setup.sh printed (PLANE_*, optional LANGFUSE_*).
```

Per-project workspaces wire the same source with `agent-sync init` in the repo
root (drop `--user`, sync renders into the project's `.claude/` etc.).

## Conventions

- **No secrets in this repo.** Use `${ENV}` placeholders; resolve locally.
- MCP set is intentionally scoped to generic dev tools + `actual-plane`.
  Other-client Plane servers and personal MCPs are deliberately excluded.
- Edit standards in `CLAUDE.md` here — never in agent-sync-rendered output
  (it's overwritten on the next sync).

-# 3. Set the secrets setup.sh printed (PLANE_*, optional LANGFUSE_*).
+`setup.sh` derives the required env vars from `mcp/*.json`, stores them in a
+environment, prompting for the rest on a TTY), and sources that file from your
+shell. Re-run it whenever a new server adds a `${VAR}` — it adds the key and

  export FEDORA=true
  export UNIX=true
fi

# Android SDK
export PATH=$PATH:~/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools/:~/.cargo/bin

# GPG TTY
export GPG_TTY=$(tty)

# Initialize Zinit if installed
if [[ -f ~/.zinit/bin/zinit.zsh ]]; then
  source ~/.zinit/bin/zinit.zsh
  
  # Load essential plugins with Zinit
  zinit light zdharma-continuum/fast-syntax-highlighting
  zinit light zsh-users/zsh-autosuggestions
  zinit light zsh-users/zsh-completions
  
  # Load a few important annexes
  zinit light-mode for \
      zdharma-continuum/zinit-annex-as-monitor \
      zdharma-continuum/zinit-annex-bin-gem-node \
      zdharma-continuum/zinit-annex-patch-dl \
      zdharma-continuum/zinit-annex-rust
  
  # Git plugin from Oh-My-Zsh
  zinit snippet OMZ::plugins/git/git.plugin.zsh
  zinit snippet OMZ::plugins/sudo/sudo.plugin.zsh
  zinit snippet OMZ::plugins/fzf/fzf.plugin.zsh
fi

# Sleep function - cross-platform
slp() {
  if [[ -x "$(command -v pmset)" ]]; then
   pmset sleepnow
  else
    echo 'Error: pmset is not installed. (NOTE: available only on macOS)'
    # On Linux/Fedora, try alternative sleep command
    if [[ -n "$FEDORA" ]]; then
      systemctl suspend
    fi
  fi
}

killport() {
    kill -15 $(lsof -i :$1 -t)
}

penv() {
    . ./venv/bin/activate
}

greb() {
  git rebase -i @^^^
}

gpob() {
  git push origin $(get_git_branch)
}

gpub() {
  git push upstream $(get_git_branch)
}

glob() {
  git pull origin $(get_git_branch)
}

glub() {
  git pull upstream $(get_git_branch)
}

get_git_branch() {
  echo $(git symbolic-ref -q --short HEAD)
}

gclone() { 
  git clone https://github.com/$GITHUB_USER/$1 $2 
}

# Modern convenience functions

# Create a new directory and enter it
mkd() {
  mkdir -p "$@" && cd "$_";
}

# Weather forecast
weather() {
  curl -s "wttr.in/${1:-}"
}

# shortcut to this dotfiles path is $ZSH
export ZSH=$HOME/.dotfiles

export ART=~/ActualReality

# your project folder that we can `c [tab]` to
export PROJECTS=~/Projects

# Stash your environment variables in ~/.localrc. This means they'll stay out
# of your main dotfiles repository (which may be public, like this one), but
# you'll have access to them in your scripts.
if [[ -a ~/.localrc ]]
then
  source ~/.localrc
fi

# all of our zsh files
typeset -U config_files
config_files=($ZSH/**/*.zsh)

# load the path files
for file in ${(M)config_files:#*/path.zsh}
do

done

# load everything but the path and completion files
for file in ${${config_files:#*/path.zsh}:#*/completion.zsh}
do

done

# initialize autocomplete here, otherwise functions won't be loaded
autoload -U compinit
compinit

# load every completion after autocomplete loads
for file in ${(M)config_files:#*/completion.zsh}
do

done

unset config_files

export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm"

content.text:
Key clarification — **the `PLANE_API_KEY` was never committed or pushed.** HEAD doesn't contain it and no commit ever touched a PLANE line; it lived only in your uncommitted working tree. So this was local plaintext-on-disk exposure only (no git leak) — **rotation is optional**, not urgent. I overstated that; correcting it.

The two blank vars (`PLANE_BASE_URL`, `PLANE_WORKSPACE_SLUG`) actually *do* have real values today — they're literals in your `~/.claude.json` `actual-plane` config, not shell exports. Let me pull them into the managed file so the migration is complete (values not printed):

input.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
import json, os, re, shlex
cc = json.load(open(os.path.expanduser('~/.claude.json')))
env = cc.get('mcpServers', {}).get('actual-plane', {}).get('env', {})
envfile = os.path.expanduser('~/.config/agent-config/env')
lines = open(envfile).read().splitlines()
def setvar(lines, var, val):
    out, done = [], False
    for ln in lines:
        if re.match(rf'^export {var}=', ln):
            out.append(f'export {var}={shlex.quote(val)}'); done = True
        else:
            out.append(ln)
    if not done:
        out.append(f'export {var}={shlex.quote(val)}')
    return out
changed = []
for v in ('PLANE_BASE_URL', 'PLANE_WORKSPACE_SLUG'):
    val = env.get(v, '')
    if val:
        lines = setvar(lines, v, val); changed.append(v)
open(envfile, 'w').write('\n'.join(lines) + '\n')
print('populated from ~/.claude.json:', changed or 'none (not present)')
PY
echo "=== managed env file (values redacted) ==="; sed -E 's/=(.+)/=<set>/' ~/.config/agent-config/env

content.text:
Done. The script-and-env-vars reconciliation is shipped and applied to your machine.

## What changed
**`setup.sh` is now drift-free** — it derives required env vars by scanning `mcp/*.json` for `${VAR}` placeholders instead of a hardcoded list. Add a server with a new secret → next run picks it up automatically. It:
- merges plugins into `~/.claude/settings.json` (backs up first),
- provisions `~/.config/agent-config/env` (0600), seeding values already in your environment and prompting for the rest on a TTY,
- sources that file from your shell, and warns on duplicate exports in shell rc files.

## Applied to your machine
- `~/.config/agent-config/env` holds all three Plane vars — `PLANE_API_KEY` (from your shell env) + `PLANE_BASE_URL` / `PLANE_WORKSPACE_SLUG` (pulled from `~/.claude.json`). Migration complete; nothing left to fill in.
- Plaintext `PLANE_API_KEY` **removed** from `~/.dotfiles/zsh/zshrc.symlink` (your `.zshrc` target). The managed file now provides it via the sourced line.
- Pushed `d4c00c1` to `Actual-Reality/agent-config`.

## Two notes
1. **No rotation needed** — I checked: that key was never committed to the dotfiles repo (HEAD doesn't have it, no commit ever touched a PLANE line), so it never left your machine via git. Earlier alarm was wrong.
2. **FYI, unrelated:** `zshrc.symlink` also has a plaintext `GEMINI_DEEP_RESEARCH_API_KEY` (line ~224) and your dotfiles working tree has ~58 lines of uncommitted local drift. Out of scope here — flagging so you can decide what to do with them. I left your dotfiles uncommitted (didn't touch beyond the one Plane line).

To activate in this shell: `source ~/.config/agent-config/env`, then restart Claude Code. New shells pick it up automatically.

"Update repos accordingly" — the repo that's now stale is the old **`claude-config`**: its README still says it's being deprecated for "tooling TBD," but that tooling (`agent-config`) now exists. Let me point it there so the team lands in the right place. Let me grab its current README first.

=== current README (full) ===
# claude-config

__DEPRECATION NOTICE: This Claude Config is being deprecated in favor of some more advanced tooling. TBD on release. You can use some of this, but I recommend taking portions of the CLAUDE.md and just using superpowers and compound engineering__

Shared [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) configuration for Actual Reality Technologies. Clone this repo as `~/.claude` to get team-wide defaults, skills, and MCP server configuration.

## Setup

### Prerequisites

- [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) CLI
- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) 20+ (for MCP servers)
- [git](https://git-scm.com/)
- [gh](https://cli.github.com/) (GitHub CLI — for `/ship`, `/review`, `pr-feedback-resolver`)
- [bun](https://bun.sh/) (for frontend projects)
- [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) (for Python projects and Plane MCP)

### Quick start

```sh
# Fresh install (or replacing an existing ~/.claude)
rm -rf ~/.claude
git clone git@github.com:Actual-Reality/claude-config.git ~/.claude
~/.claude/scripts/setup.sh
```

The setup script installs:

| Component | What it does |
|---|---|
| **context7** MCP | Library/framework docs lookup |
| **playwright** MCP | Browser automation (replaces gstack browse) |
| **chrome-devtools** MCP | Performance traces, memory snapshots, Lighthouse |
| **sequential-thinking** MCP | Complex multi-step reasoning |
| **plane** MCP | Time tracking via Plane API |
| **superpowers** plugin | Planning, brainstorming, TDD, code review workflows |
| **pr-review-toolkit** plugin | PR review agents |
| **code-review** plugin | Code review agents |
| **feature-dev** plugin | Feature development agents |
| **frontend-design** plugin | Frontend design agents |
| **security-guidance** plugin | Security review agents |
| **github** plugin | GitHub integration |
| **typescript-lsp** plugin | TypeScript language server |
| **gopls-lsp** plugin | Go language server |
| **update-check** hook | Auto-pulls latest config on session start (hourly) |
| **settings.json** | Created with default hooks (if not present) |

The script is idempotent — run it again anytime to pick up new requirements.

### Plane time tracking (optional)

Create `~/.claude/.plane`:

```
workspace_slug=actual
api_url=https://plane.actualreality.ai
api_key_env=PLANE_API_KEY
```

Export the API key in your shell profile:

```bash
export PLANE_API_KEY=plane_api_<your-key>
```

### Project-specific overrides

Each repo can have its own `CLAUDE.md` that extends or overrides these defaults.

## What's included

**`CLAUDE.md`** — Team-wide defaults: workflow, architecture, stack, testing, conventions.

**`commands/`** — Custom slash commands: `/commit-push-pr`, `/start-timer`, `/stop-timer`, `/tracking-status`.

**`scripts/`** — Setup and maintenance:
- `setup.sh` — One-time bootstrap (MCP servers, plugins, hooks)
- `update-check.sh` — SessionStart hook for auto-updates

## Skills

**`skills/plane-time-tracking/`** — Session time tracking via [Plane](https://plane.so) API. Parses branch names to auto-detect issues, starts/stops timers, handles orphaned sessions.

**`skills/react-nextjs/`** — React 19 + Next.js 15 code patterns. Server/Client Components, App Router, TanStack Query, Zustand, Zod forms.

**`skills/pr-feedback-resolver/`** — Multi-agent PR review comment resolution. Evaluates, deduplicates, fixes or dismisses with rationale, replies and resolves threads. Requires `gh` CLI.

**`skills/ui-research-design/`** — Research real-world UI patterns and synthesize them into distinctive, implementable designs. Bridges the gap between "I want something unique" and actual implementation. Use before or alongside `creative-frontend-design`.

**`skills/gstack/`** — 24 skills adapted from [gstack](https://github.com/garrytan/gstack) for the post-build lifecycle:

| Category | Skills |
|---|---|
| Review | `/review`, `/codex`, `/cso` |
| Design | `/design-review`, `/plan-design-review`, `/design-consultation`, `/design-shotgun`, `/design-html` |
| DX | `/devex-review`, `/plan-devex-review` |
| Product | `/office-hours` |
| QA | `/qa`, `/qa-only` |
| Ship | `/ship`, `/land-and-deploy`, `/canary`, `/document-release` |
| Debug | `/investigate` |
| Reflect | `/retro`, `/learn` |
| Safety | `/careful`, `/freeze`, `/unfreeze`, `/guard` |

Browser interaction uses Playwright MCP instead of the gstack browse binary. See `skills/gstack/BROWSER.md` for the command mapping.

content.text:

# claude-config — superseded

> ## ➡️ Moved to [Actual-Reality/agent-config](https://github.com/Actual-Reality/agent-config)
>
> `agent-config` is the canonical AI-agent configuration for Actual Reality,
> consumed by [`agent-sync`](https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync). It
> replaces the old "clone this repo as `~/.claude`" model with a Git-backed
> source that `agent-sync` renders into each tool's native files (Claude Code,
> Cursor, Codex) — so you no longer overwrite your whole `~/.claude`.
>
> **Setup (see agent-config's README for detail):**
> ```sh
> git clone git@github.com:Actual-Reality/agent-config.git
> bash agent-config/scripts/setup.sh        # plugins + MCP env vars
> agent-sync init --source https://github.com/Actual-Reality/agent-config \
>   --ref main --target claude
> agent-sync sync --user                    # renders into ~/.claude
> ```
>
> **Do not clone this repo as `~/.claude`.** The content below is retained for
> reference only and is no longer maintained.

---

Shared [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) configuration for Actual Reality Technologies (legacy — see above).

# claude-config

__DEPRECATION NOTICE: This Claude Config is being deprecated in favor of some more advanced tooling. TBD on release. You can use some of this, but I recommend taking portions of the CLAUDE.md and just using superpowers and compound engineering__

Shared [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) configuration for Actual Reality Technologies. Clone this repo as `~/.claude` to get team-wide defaults, skills, and MCP server configuration.

## Setup

### Prerequisites

- [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) CLI
- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) 20+ (for MCP servers)
- [git](https://git-scm.com/)
- [gh](https://cli.github.com/) (GitHub CLI — for `/ship`, `/review`, `pr-feedback-resolver`)
- [bun](https://bun.sh/) (for frontend projects)
- [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) (for Python projects and Plane MCP)

### Quick start

```sh
# Fresh install (or replacing an existing ~/.claude)
rm -rf ~/.claude
git clone git@github.com:Actual-Reality/claude-config.git ~/.claude
~/.claude/scripts/setup.sh
```

The setup script installs:

| Component | What it does |
|---|---|
| **context7** MCP | Library/framework docs lookup |
| **playwright** MCP | Browser automation (replaces gstack browse) |
| **chrome-devtools** MCP | Performance traces, memory snapshots, Lighthouse |
| **sequential-thinking** MCP | Complex multi-step reasoning |
| **plane** MCP | Time tracking via Plane API |
| **superpowers** plugin | Planning, brainstorming, TDD, code review workflows |
| **pr-review-toolkit** plugin | PR review agents |
| **code-review** plugin | Code review agents |
| **feature-dev** plugin | Feature development agents |
| **frontend-design** plugin | Frontend design agents |
| **security-guidance** plugin | Security review agents |
| **github** plugin | GitHub integration |
| **typescript-lsp** plugin | TypeScript language server |
| **gopls-lsp** plugin | Go language server |
| **update-check** hook | Auto-pulls latest config on session start (hourly) |
| **settings.json** | Created with default hooks (if not present) |

The script is idempotent — run it again anytime to pick up new requirements.

### Plane time tracking (optional)

Create `~/.claude/.plane`:

```
workspace_slug=actual
api_url=https://plane.actualreality.ai
api_key_env=PLANE_API_KEY
```

Export the API key in your shell profile:

```bash
export PLANE_API_KEY=plane_api_<your-key>
```

### Project-specific overrides

Each repo can have its own `CLAUDE.md` that extends or overrides these defaults.

## What's included

**`CLAUDE.md`** — Team-wide defaults: workflow, architecture, stack, testing, conventions.

**`commands/`** — Custom slash commands: `/commit-push-pr`, `/start-timer`, `/stop-timer`, `/tracking-status`.

**`scripts/`** — Setup and maintenance:
- `setup.sh` — One-time bootstrap (MCP servers, plugins, hooks)
- `update-check.sh` — SessionStart hook for auto-updates

## Skills

**`skills/plane-time-tracking/`** — Session time tracking via [Plane](https://plane.so) API. Parses branch names to auto-detect issues, starts/stops timers, handles orphaned sessions.

**`skills/react-nextjs/`** — React 19 + Next.js 15 code patterns. Server/Client Components, App Router, TanStack Query, Zustand, Zod forms.

**`skills/pr-feedback-resolver/`** — Multi-agent PR review comment resolution. Evaluates, deduplicates, fixes or dismisses with rationale, replies and resolves threads. Requires `gh` CLI.

**`skills/ui-research-design/`** — Research real-world UI patterns and synthesize them into distinctive, implementable designs. Bridges the gap between "I want something unique" and actual implementation. Use before or alongside `creative-frontend-design`.

**`skills/gstack/`** — 24 skills adapted from [gstack](https://github.com/garrytan/gstack) for the post-build lifecycle:

| Category | Skills |
|---|---|
| Review | `/review`, `/codex`, `/cso` |
| Design | `/design-review`, `/plan-design-review`, `/design-consultation`, `/design-shotgun`, `/design-html` |
| DX | `/devex-review`, `/plan-devex-review` |
| Product | `/office-hours` |
| QA | `/qa`, `/qa-only` |
| Ship | `/ship`, `/land-and-deploy`, `/canary`, `/document-release` |
| Debug | `/investigate` |
| Reflect | `/retro`, `/learn` |
| Safety | `/careful`, `/freeze`, `/unfreeze`, `/guard` |

Browser interaction uses Playwright MCP instead of the gstack browse binary. See `skills/gstack/BROWSER.md` for the command mapping.

Done — `claude-config` updated via **[PR #5](https://github.com/Actual-Reality/claude-config/pull/5)** (its `pull_request` rule means it needs a merge, so I opened a PR rather than bypassing). It rewrites the README as a clear redirect to `agent-config`, with new setup steps, and stops telling people to clone it as `~/.claude`.

## Repo status after this turn
| Repo | State |
|------|-------|
| **`Actual-Reality/agent-config`** | ✅ canonical source, current (`d4c00c1`) |
| **`Actual-Reality/claude-config`** | 📝 PR #5 open — superseded-redirect, ready for your merge |
| **`justinbeaudry/dotfiles`** | ⚠️ left untouched by me — see below |
| **`~/.claude`** (git) | left as-is (4 behind `claude-config`) |

## What I deliberately did *not* touch
- **dotfiles** — your working tree already had unrelated drift (`bin/dot` deleted, `git/gitignore.symlink`, `zsh/aliases.zsh` modified) plus my one-line `zshrc.symlink` edit. Committing would bundle all of that. That's your personal repo and your call — I won't fold my Plane-key removal in with unrelated changes.
- **`~/.claude`** git repo — it tracks `claude-config` and is 4 behind with local deletions. Now that the model is agent-sync-based, this clone is essentially legacy. Touching its git state risks your live config dir.

Two things I can do if you want them — tell me which:
1. **dotfiles** — commit *only* the `zshrc.symlink` Plane-key removal (I'll stage just that file's hunk, leaving your other drift alone). Note: that file also still has a plaintext `GEMINI_DEEP_RESEARCH_API_KEY`.
2. **Plane defaults** — since `PLANE_BASE_URL`/`PLANE_WORKSPACE_SLUG` are non-secret team values (`https://plane.actualreality.ai` / `actual`), I can bake them into `mcp/actual-plane.json` as `${VAR:-default}` so only `PLANE_API_KEY` ever needs provisioning. Cleaner for teammates.

input.description: Verify Claude Code config read paths
input.prompt:
I need precise, authoritative answers about where Claude Code (the CLI) reads its configuration, to decide how to wire a managed config setup. Please answer each with specifics (and cite docs where possible):

1. **Memory / CLAUDE.md lookup order.** Exactly which CLAUDE.md files does Claude Code load, and in what precedence? Specifically: does it read `~/CLAUDE.md` (directly in the home directory), or only `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` for user-global memory? Does it walk up parent directories from cwd to `/` and load a `CLAUDE.md` at each level (which would include `~/CLAUDE.md` when working under home)?

2. **MCP server config locations & scopes.** Where are MCP servers stored for each scope (local / project / user)? Specifically: is user-scope MCP stored in `~/.claude.json`? Is `~/.mcp.json` ever read, or only a project-root `.mcp.json` when you launch Claude in that project directory? If I write MCP servers to `~/.mcp.json`, will global Claude sessions (launched from arbitrary project dirs) pick them up?

3. **Environment variable expansion in MCP config.** Does Claude Code expand `${VAR}` and `${VAR:-default}` placeholders in MCP server `env`/`args`/`url` values? Does that expansion work in BOTH a project `.mcp.json` AND the user-level `~/.claude.json` mcpServers block? Where do the variable values come from — the shell environment Claude Code is launched from?

Keep it tight and factual. I specifically need to know whether rendering to `~/CLAUDE.md` and `~/.mcp.json` would actually be picked up by global Claude Code sessions, vs needing `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and `~/.claude.json`.

content.text:
Excellent. Now I have the authoritative docs. Here are your precise answers:

---

## 1. **Memory / CLAUDE.md lookup order**

Claude Code **walks up the directory tree from your current working directory**. It does NOT read `~/CLAUDE.md` (a file directly in the home directory).

**Load order (filesystem root down to cwd):**
- Managed policy CLAUDE.md (e.g., `/Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/CLAUDE.md` on macOS)
- `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (user scope — always checked regardless of cwd)
- Any `CLAUDE.md` or `CLAUDE.local.md` files in ancestor directories up the tree from cwd
- `./CLAUDE.md` or `./.claude/CLAUDE.md` (project scope, at cwd)
- `./CLAUDE.local.md` (local scope, at cwd)

**Key detail:** When you launch Claude from `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/`, it walks up: → `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/` → `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/` → `/Users/justinbeaudry/` → `/Users/` → `/`. At each level, it checks for `CLAUDE.md` and `CLAUDE.local.md`. It does NOT automatically pick up a file at `~/CLAUDE.md` *unless* your cwd is under `~` and the walk passes through it.

**Rendering to `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` WILL be picked up by global Claude sessions** — it's always read as the user-scope file, regardless of where you launch from.

---

## 2. **MCP server config locations & scopes**

| Scope | Location | Applied to |
|-------|----------|-----------|
| **User** | `~/.claude.json` | All projects (loaded globally) |
| **Project** | `.mcp.json` (in repo root) | This project only |

**Critical:** There is **no `~/.mcp.json`**. User-level MCP is stored only in `~/.claude.json`.

**Rendering to `~/.claude.json` WILL be picked up by global Claude sessions** — it's the authoritative user-scope MCP file for all projects.

The precedence order for MCP config is:
1. Project `.mcp.json` (highest priority)
2. User `~/.claude.json` (fallback)

---

## 3. **Environment variable expansion in MCP config**

The docs do not explicitly specify whether Claude Code expands `${VAR}` and `${VAR:-default}` placeholders in MCP `env`/`args`/`url` fields. The MCP servers guide discusses *configuring* MCP servers but does not document variable substitution syntax.

**Likely behavior based on MCP standard:** The Model Context Protocol spec allows environment variable expansion in MCP server definitions, but the Claude Code docs don't guarantee it at both scopes. To be safe, verify with a test:

1. Set an env var in your shell.
2. Add MCP config with `${VAR}` placeholder to `~/.claude.json`.
3. Launch Claude Code from a fresh shell and check if the variable expanded.

If it doesn't work, you'll need to inline values or use shell expansion before writing the JSON.

---

## Summary for your managed config setup

**Use `~/.claude.json` and `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`** to render user-global config. Both will be picked up by Claude Code sessions launched from any directory. **Do not use `~/CLAUDE.md` or `~/.mcp.json`** — they are not standard locations and won't be reliably loaded.

**Sources:**
- [Claude Code Settings Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/configuration.md)
- [Claude Code Memory / CLAUDE.md](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory.md)
- [Claude Code MCP Servers](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp-servers.md)

- **Claude reads `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`**, not `~/CLAUDE.md`.

content.text:
That's the full picture — and the answer is: **your machine is already running the new setup.** Nothing more is required.

| Piece | Status on your machine |
|-------|------------------------|
| **Plugins** | ✅ all 6 enabled + marketplaces registered in `~/.claude/settings.json` (via `setup.sh`) |
| **Env vars** | ✅ `~/.config/agent-config/env` present (0600), sourced from `.zshrc` |
| **MCP servers** | ✅ live in `~/.claude.json` (they were the *source* for agent-config) |
| **Global standards** | ✅ your `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (141 lines) is a superset of the 62-line team starter — nothing to import |

**To activate in-session:** restart Claude Code (picks up the newly-enabled plugins), and open a new shell or `source ~/.config/agent-config/env` (so MCP env vars are live).

So agent-config didn't need to *push* anything new to you — your machine *is* the reference. agent-config is the **reproducible path for teammates and new machines**: clone → `setup.sh` → (optionally) `agent-sync` per-project.

## Two things worth knowing

1. **`sync --user` is a dead end for Claude globally** — agent-sync's Claude adapter writes `~/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.mcp.json` at user scope, but Claude reads `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.claude.json`. Since you own agent-sync, that's a real adapter gap worth a future fix (make user-scope target `~/.claude/`). Until then, use it at **project** scope, where `./CLAUDE.md`/`./.mcp.json` *are* read.

2. **One optional cleanup:** your `~/.claude.json` still has `PLANE_API_KEY` as a **literal** secret. I can swap it to `${PLANE_API_KEY}` so it pulls from the env file (matching the team model, no duplicate secret) — but only if Claude Code expands `${VAR}` in `~/.claude.json`, which the docs don't confirm. Want me to test that and convert it if it works? (It's local-only and not leaked, so low urgency either way.)

Net: there's nothing blocking you — restart Claude Code and you're on it.

input.command:
set -e
# Ensure the managed env (PLANE_API_KEY) is loaded into this process for the health-check.

# Backup the full user config.

ls -t ~/.claude.json.bak.* | head -1 | sed 's/.*/backup: &/'
# Swap the literal secret for the placeholder (only actual-plane PLANE_API_KEY).
python3 - <<'PY'
import json, os
p = os.path.expanduser('~/.claude.json')
d = json.load(open(p))
env = d['mcpServers']['actual-plane']['env']
before = env['PLANE_API_KEY']
env['PLANE_API_KEY'] = '${PLANE_API_KEY}'
json.dump(d, open(p,'w'), indent=2)
print('was literal:', before.startswith('plane_api'))
print('now:', env['PLANE_API_KEY'])
PY

sleep 1
echo "=== probe result ==="; cat /tmp/probe.out 2>&1
# Clean up the throwaway server.
claude mcp remove envtest -s user 2>&1 | head -1

**Confirmed — expansion works at user scope.** The probe proves it directly:
- `${TEST_EXPAND}` → `EXPANDED_OK` (expanded from env)
- `${TEST_DEF:-DEFAULT_OK}` → `DEFAULT_OK` (default form works too)

Both resolved in `~/.claude.json`. So **#2 is done and verified**: your `~/.claude.json` `actual-plane` now uses `${PLANE_API_KEY}` (literal secret removed), resolving from the sourced env file. The earlier "Connected" was a red herring — `plane-mcp-server` validates lazily — but the probe is conclusive.

Net for #2: the secret no longer sits duplicated in `~/.claude.json`; it lives only in the 0600 env file. Backup saved at `~/.claude.json.bak.*` if you want to diff.

content.text: I'll take it, plan-first. Let me investigate the fix surface in the code before writing the plan — specifically how tool-owned paths are declared, whether scope (user/project/dir) is available at emit time, and where relative paths get rooted.

content.content:
1	package claude
2	
3	import (
4		_ "embed"
5		"fmt"
6	
7		"github.com/goccy/go-yaml"
8	
9		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/capmatrix"
10		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/ir"
11		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/pkg/adapterkit"
12	)
13	
14	//go:embed capabilities.yaml
15	var capabilitiesYAML []byte
16	
17	// declarationFile is the parsed shape of capabilities.yaml. It is
18	// intentionally narrower than adapter.AdapterManifest so the file
19	// stays a per-adapter declaration and not an arbitrary manifest
20	// override.
21	type declarationFile struct {
22		Name            string                            `yaml:"name"`
23		Version         string                            `yaml:"version"`
24		ContractVersion string                            `yaml:"contract_version"`
25		ReservedPrefix  string                            `yaml:"reserved_prefix"`
26		ConceptKinds    map[string]conceptKindDeclaration `yaml:"concept_kinds"`
27	}
28	
29	type conceptKindDeclaration struct {
30		Status capmatrix.CapabilityStatus `yaml:"status"`
31		Note   string                     `yaml:"note,omitempty"`
32	}
33	
34	// conceptKinds is the in-code mirror of capabilities.yaml. The unit
35	// test asserts the two are kept in sync; if you add a kind here,
36	// add it there (and vice versa).
37	//
38	// The status values use capmatrix's vocabulary; the wire conversion
39	// happens in capabilitiesForWire.
40	var conceptKinds = map[ir.Kind]capmatrix.CapabilityStatus{
41		ir.KindAgentsMD:        capmatrix.Supported,
42		ir.KindRule:            capmatrix.Supported,
43		ir.KindSkill:           capmatrix.Supported,
44		ir.KindCommand:         capmatrix.Supported,
45		ir.KindMCPServerEntry:  capmatrix.Supported,
46		ir.KindPluginReference: capmatrix.Unsupported,
47	}
48	
49	// capabilitiesForWire returns the adapterkit.Capabilities the adapter
50	// echoes in its initialize response. WriteToolOwned is true because
51	// the claude adapter emits write_tool_owned ops for .mcp.json and
52	// CLAUDE.md.
53	func capabilitiesForWire() adapterkit.Capabilities {
54		builder := adapterkit.NewCapabilities().WithWriteToolOwned(true)
55		for kind, status := range conceptKinds {
56			switch status {
57			case capmatrix.Supported:
58				builder.Supports(string(kind))
59			case capmatrix.Partial:
60				builder.Partial(string(kind))
61			case capmatrix.Unsupported:
62				builder.Unsupported(string(kind))
63			}
64		}
65		return builder.Build()
66	}
67	
68	// declaredOutputs returns the list the adapter advertises in its
69	// initialize response. The runtime path-safety gate
70	// (internal/adapter/runtime.go pathInDeclaredOutputs) accepts an
71	// emitted op only when its path falls inside one of these declared
72	// outputs.
73	func declaredOutputs() []adapterkit.DeclaredOutput {
74		mcpPointer := "/mcpServers"
75		claudeMDSection := "agent-sync"
76		return []adapterkit.DeclaredOutput{
77			{Path: ".claude/rules/agent-sync", Mode: adapterkit.OutputModeOwnedSubdir},
78			{Path: ".claude/commands/agent-sync", Mode: adapterkit.OutputModeOwnedSubdir},
79			// .claude/skills holds agent-sync-<id> leaf dirs alongside the user's own
80			// skills. shared-subdir → manage only our leaves, never the parent, so
81			// user skills survive a sync. (.claude/rules/agent-sync and
82			// .claude/commands/agent-sync are agent-sync-exclusive, so they stay
83			// owned-subdir and are swapped wholesale.)
84			{Path: ".claude/skills", Mode: adapterkit.OutputModeSharedSubdir},
85			{Path: ".mcp.json", Mode: adapterkit.OutputModeToolOwnedEntry, JSONPointer: &mcpPointer},
86			{Path: "CLAUDE.md", Mode: adapterkit.OutputModeToolOwnedEntry, SectionID: &claudeMDSection},
87			// The strict-JSON sidecar marker (.agent-sync-managed) is written
88			// next to .mcp.json at workspace root. Declared as
89			// owned-subdir on "." would over-broadly authorize the whole
90			// workspace, so we declare the sidecar file by exact path.
91			{Path: ".agent-sync-managed", Mode: adapterkit.OutputModeOwnedSubdir},
92		}
93	}
94	
95	// parseCapabilitiesYAML decodes the embedded YAML. Used by the parity
96	// test and by callers that want the human-readable note bodies.
97	func parseCapabilitiesYAML(src []byte) (declarationFile, error) {
98		var d declarationFile
99		if err := yaml.UnmarshalWithOptions(
100			src,
101			&d,
102			yaml.DisallowUnknownField(),
103			yaml.AllowFieldPrefixes("x-"),
104		); err != nil {
105			return declarationFile{}, fmt.Errorf("claude: parse capabilities.yaml: %s", yaml.FormatError(err, false, true))
106		}
107		return d, nil
108	}
109	
110	// loadDeclaration parses the embedded capabilities.yaml and returns
111	// the result. Cached at package level if it ever becomes a hot path;
112	// for now the call sites are test-only and one-off.
113	func loadDeclaration() (declarationFile, error) {
114		return parseCapabilitiesYAML(capabilitiesYAML)
115	}
116
