content.text: There's already a plan file dated today — `2026-06-18-001-fix-claude-user-scope-paths-plan.md`. Let me read it before writing anything; you may have already started this.

content.content:
1 --- 2 title: "fix: scope-aware Claude adapter output paths (sync --user targets ~/.claude)" 3 status: draft 4 date: 2026-06-18 5 type: fix 6 origin: docs/plans/2026-06-17-002-feat-multi-scope-sync-plan.md 7 master_plan: docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md 8 --- 9 10 # fix: scope-aware Claude adapter output paths (sync --user targets ~/.claude) 11 12 ## Summary 13 14 `sync --user` writes two Claude outputs to paths Claude Code never reads. 15 The Claude adapter declares and emits **workspace-relative** paths 16 (`CLAUDE.md`, `.mcp.json`) that are correct at project scope but wrong at 17 user scope: at `--user` the scope root is the home directory 18 (`internal/hierarchy/discover.go:108`, `Root = home`), so they resolve to 19 `~/CLAUDE.md` and `~/.mcp.json`. Claude Code reads user-global config from 20 `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and `~/.claude.json`. The files agent-sync writes at 21 user scope are inert. 22 23 The asymmetry is the tell: the adapter's other outputs 24 (`.claude/rules/agent-sync`, `.claude/commands/agent-sync`, `.claude/skills`) 25 already live under `.claude/`, so they land correctly at `~/.claude/...`. 26 Only the two **root-level tool-owned-entry** outputs are misplaced — and 27 `.mcp.json → ~/.claude.json` is a *filename* change, not a directory-prefix 28 swap, so no generic prefix rewrite fixes it. 29 30 This plan makes the Claude adapter **scope-aware**: the adapter learns the 31 scope level (user / project / directory) at `initialize`, and at user scope 32 declares + emits `.claude/CLAUDE.md` and `.claude.json` instead of the 33 project-scope paths. The scope signal is threaded as an additive field 34 through the existing handshake. Project and directory scope behavior is 35 unchanged. 36 37 --- 38 39 ## Problem Frame 40 41 **Confirmed dead-end write at user scope.** Verified by code trace 42 (2026-06-18): 43 44 - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/capabilities.go:73` `declaredOutputs()` 45 returns static workspace-relative paths, including 46 `{Path: "CLAUDE.md", Mode: ToolOwnedEntry, SectionID: "agent-sync"}` and 47 `{Path: ".mcp.json", Mode: ToolOwnedEntry, JSONPointer: "/mcpServers"}`. 48 - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit_tool_owned.go:12-15` hardcodes the 49 same constants (`mcpJSONPath = ".mcp.json"`, `claudeMDPath = "CLAUDE.md"`, 50 `claudeMDSidecar = ".agent-sync-managed"`); `emitMCPServerEntry` and 51 `emitAgentsMD` emit ops at those literals. 52 - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/bundled.go` `OnInitialize` **discards** 53 its `InitializeParams` (`func(_, _ adapterkit.InitializeParams)`) and 54 returns the static `declaredOutputs()` — so even though the handshake 55 already carries `WorkspaceRoot`, the adapter ignores it. 56 - `internal/cli/setup.go:107-116` builds one `engine.Request` per scope with 57 `WorkspacePath: scopeRoot`; at user scope `scopeRoot = home` 58 (`internal/hierarchy/discover.go:108`). `internal/engine/target.go:46` 59 passes that straight into `adapter.SessionOptions.WorkspaceRoot`. 60 - Claude Code reads user-global instructions from `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and 61 user-scope MCP servers from `~/.claude.json` — neither of which the 62 adapter targets. 63 64 Net effect: `sync --user` succeeds, reports success, and writes 65 `~/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.mcp.json` that Claude never loads. 66 67 The path-safety gate already enforces the link that makes this fixable as a 68 single coordinated change: `internal/adapter/runtime.go:500` 69 `pathInDeclaredOutputs` accepts an emitted op only if its path is contained 70 in a declared output. So declared outputs and emitted paths must move 71 together — change one without the other and the gate rejects the emit. The 72 existing in-code/YAML mirror invariant in `capabilities.go:34-36` is the same 73 discipline. 74 75 --- 76 77 ## Requirements Trace 78 79 - **R-US1** — At user scope, the Claude adapter declares and emits the 80 agents-md overlay at `.claude/CLAUDE.md` (relative to the home root), and a 81 `sync --user` writes the managed section there. → U2, U3, U4 82 - **R-US2** — At user scope, the Claude adapter declares and emits the 83 mcp-server-entry at `.claude.json` (relative to the home root), merging into 84 the `/mcpServers/agentsync_` pointer and preserving all unrelated keys 85 in that file. → U2, U3, U4 86 - **R-US3** — At **project** and **directory** scope, declared outputs and 87 emitted paths are byte-for-byte unchanged (`CLAUDE.md`, `.mcp.json`); no 88 regression to today's behavior. → U2, U3, U4 89 - **R-US4** — The scope signal is an **additive, backward-compatible** 90 extension to the initialize handshake: an adapter that ignores it defaults 91 to project-scope paths; an absent signal is treated as project scope. → U1 92 - **R-US5** — The `.claude/...` owned-subdir / shared-subdir outputs 93 (rules, commands, skills) are unchanged at every scope (they already land 94 correctly under `~/.claude/`). → U2 95 - **R-US6** — The `.agent-sync-managed` strict-JSON sidecar behaves 96 coherently at user scope (see Open Question OQ-1): it is **not** written 97 next to `~/.claude.json` as if agent-sync owned that shared file. → U3 98 99 Success criteria: a `sync --user` for Claude writes `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and 100 merges into `~/.claude.json` (preserving foreign keys); project/directory 101 syncs are unchanged; `go test -race ./...`, `golangci-lint run`, and the 80% 102 coverage floor stay green. 103 104 --- 105 106 ## High-Level Technical Design 107 108 The adapter is the sole owner of Claude's on-disk path knowledge (the 109 architecture's gateway/adapter boundary: core never encodes per-tool paths). 110 So the fix lives in the adapter — the engine must only **tell the adapter 111 which scope it is emitting for**. Today the handshake gives the adapter 112 `WorkspaceRoot` but not the scope *level*; inferring "is this home?" from the 113 root is fragile (symlinked homes, `--home` override in tests, nested repos at 114 `$HOME`). We pass the level explicitly. 115 116 ```mermaid 117 flowchart TD 118 A["hierarchy.Scope.Level
(user / project / directory)"] --> B["engine.Request.Scope
(new field)"] 119 B --> C["adapter.SessionOptions.Scope"] 120 C --> D["InitializeParams.Scope
(new additive field, omitempty)"] 121 D --> E["claude OnInitialize:
capture scope into session state"] 122 E --> F{scope == user?} 123 F -->|yes| G["declaredOutputs(user):
.claude/CLAUDE.md + .claude.json"] 124 F -->|no / absent| H["declaredOutputs(project):
CLAUDE.md + .mcp.json (unchanged)"] 125 E --> I["claude OnEmit:
resolve paths from captured scope"] 126 I --> F2{scope == user?} 127 F2 -->|yes| J["emit agents-md -> .claude/CLAUDE.md
emit mcp-entry -> .claude.json
suppress root sidecar (OQ-1)"] 128 F2 -->|no| K["emit -> CLAUDE.md / .mcp.json (unchanged)"] 129 G -.path-safety gate.-> J 130 H -.path-safety gate.-> K 131 ``` 132 133 **Key insight:** the bundled adapter's `run()` constructs a fresh 134 `adapterkit.Server` per session, so `OnInitialize` and `OnEmit` can close over 135 a single per-session `scope` variable. `declaredOutputs` and the emit helpers 136 become functions of scope; everything else (tool-owned-entry merge machinery, 137 JSON-pointer merge, markdown-section merge, the path-safety gate) is reused 138 unchanged — only the *paths* the adapter chooses differ. 139 140 --- 141 142 ## Key Technical Decisions 143 144 - **Scope is carried by an additive `Scope` field on the initialize 145 handshake, not inferred from `WorkspaceRoot`.** Inference is unreliable 146 (symlinked/overridden homes, a repo whose `.git` sits at `$HOME`). A 147 first-class string enum (`"user"` | `"project"` | `"directory"`) on 148 `InitializeParams` (contract + adapterkit, `omitempty`) is explicit, 149 schema-parity-testable, and backward compatible: absent ⇒ project scope. 150 This follows the project's "freeze the frame, grow capabilities" policy — 151 the same additive shape used when `shared-subdir` was added (no version 152 bump). *Alternative considered:* ride through the existing 153 `InitializeParams.Meta` (`_meta`) raw field with no typed surface — rejected 154 as less discoverable and untestable via schema parity. (See OQ-2.) 155 - **The fix is in the adapter, not an engine-side path rewrite.** Having the 156 engine remap `.mcp.json → .claude.json` / `CLAUDE.md → .claude/CLAUDE.md` 157 for user scope would push per-tool, per-scope filename knowledge into core, 158 violating the adapter-owns-its-tool's-paths boundary. The `.mcp.json → 159 .claude.json` *filename* change (not a prefix move) makes a generic rewrite 160 impossible anyway. 161 - **`.claude.json` is merged surgically, never owned wholesale.** At user 162 scope the MCP target is Claude's live combined config (`~/.claude.json` holds 163 projects, history, auth, MRU state — far more than `mcpServers`). The 164 existing `tool-owned-entry` JSON-pointer merge writes only 165 `/mcpServers/agentsync_` and preserves every other key — exactly the 166 right primitive. The risk is higher than `.mcp.json` (a bug corrupts a 167 load-bearing file), so U4 includes a foreign-key-preservation test against a 168 realistic `.claude.json` fixture. 169 - **Default-to-project keeps all existing call sites correct.** The 170 single-scope path (`prepareEngine`, used by `validate` and explicit 171 `--workspace` sync) passes `LevelProject`; an absent wire field also means 172 project. No existing test or behavior shifts unless `--user` is in play. 173 - **Scope-awareness is a general adapter capability, applied here only to 174 Claude.** The wire field is adapter-agnostic, but this plan changes only the 175 Claude adapter. Cursor (`.cursor/...`) and Codex are out of scope (see 176 Scope Boundaries — Codex's `AGENTS.md` at user scope is a separate question). 177 178 --- 179 180 ## Implementation Units 181 182 ### U1. Thread a `Scope` signal through the initialize handshake 183 184 **Goal:** Add an additive `Scope` field to the initialize params across the 185 SDK type, the wire-contract mirror, and the JSON schema, and plumb it from the 186 hierarchy scope level down to the adapter session. 187 188 **Requirements:** R-US4. 189 190 **Dependencies:** none. 191 192 **Files:** 193 - `pkg/adapterkit/types.go` — add `Scope string` to `InitializeParams` 194 (`json:"scope,omitempty"`), with a doc comment enumerating 195 `user`/`project`/`directory` and "absent ⇒ project". 196 - `internal/adapter/contract/protocol.go` — mirror the field on the contract 197 `InitializeParams` (after `IRVersion`, before `Meta`). 198 - `internal/adapter/contract/schema/*initialize*.json` — add the optional 199 `scope` property with the enum, if initialize params are schema-validated. 200 - `internal/adapter/adapter.go` (`SessionOptions`) — add a `Scope` field. 201 - `internal/adapter/runtime.go` `Initialize` — set `params.Scope` from 202 `s.options.Scope` (default `"project"` when empty). 203 - `internal/engine/request.go` — add `Scope string` (or `hierarchy.Level`) to 204 `Request`; `internal/engine/target.go:46` and 205 `internal/engine/capability.go:45` pass it into `SessionOptions`. 206 - `internal/cli/setup.go` — `prepareScope` gains a scope-level parameter; the 207 hierarchy orchestrator passes each `Scope.Level`, and `prepareEngine` passes 208 `LevelProject`. Map `hierarchy.Level.String()` → the wire value. 209 - Tests: `pkg/adapterkit/types_test.go`, 210 `internal/adapter/contract/*_test.go`, the schema-parity test, and 211 `internal/adapter/runtime_test.go`. 212 213 **Approach:** Purely additive. The level already has a stable lowercase 214 `String()` (`internal/hierarchy/hierarchy.go`: `user`/`project`/`directory`), 215 so reuse it as the wire value rather than inventing a parallel enum. Confirm 216 schema-parity (SDK ↔ contract ↔ JSON schema) holds with the new optional 217 field, and that an initialize payload omitting `scope` still validates. 218 219 **Patterns to follow:** the existing `IRVersion` / `ReservedPrefix` fields on 220 `InitializeParams` and their round-trip + parity coverage; the `shared-subdir` 221 additive-enum precedent in `docs/plans/2026-06-09-003-...`. 222 223 **Test scenarios:** 224 - `InitializeParams{Scope: "user"}` round-trips through marshal/unmarshal and 225 validates against the schema. 226 - An initialize payload with no `scope` key validates and decodes to `""` 227 (runtime treats it as project). 228 - Schema parity: SDK, contract, and JSON-schema agree on the field + enum. 229 - `runtime.Initialize` sends `scope: "project"` when `SessionOptions.Scope` 230 is empty (backward-compat default). 231 232 **Verification:** parity + round-trip tests pass; the field exists in all 233 three layers and is populated by the engine per scope. 234 235 ### U2. Make the Claude adapter's declared outputs scope-aware 236 237 **Goal:** `declaredOutputs` returns `.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `.claude.json` at 238 user scope and the current `CLAUDE.md` + `.mcp.json` at project/directory 239 scope; capture the scope in `OnInitialize`. 240 241 **Requirements:** R-US1, R-US2, R-US3, R-US5. 242 243 **Dependencies:** U1. 244 245 **Files:** 246 - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/bundled.go` — `OnInitialize` reads 247 `params.Scope` and stores it in a per-session variable closed over by both 248 handlers; pass scope into `declaredOutputs(scope)`. 249 - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/capabilities.go` — `declaredOutputs` takes 250 the scope and selects the two tool-owned-entry paths accordingly. The 251 `.claude/rules/agent-sync`, `.claude/commands/agent-sync`, `.claude/skills`, 252 and sidecar entries are unaffected at project scope; sidecar handling at 253 user scope per U3/OQ-1. 254 - Tests: `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/capabilities_test.go`. 255 256 **Approach:** Introduce a small `scope` type (or reuse the wire string) and a 257 `pathSet` helper that resolves the two tool-owned paths from scope. Keep the 258 JSON pointer (`/mcpServers`) and section id (`agent-sync`) identical across 259 scopes — only `Path` changes. Default/unknown scope ⇒ project paths. 260 261 **Patterns to follow:** the current `declaredOutputs()` shape and the 262 in-code/YAML mirror discipline (`capabilities.go:34`). 263 264 **Test scenarios:** 265 - `declaredOutputs("user")` lists `.claude/CLAUDE.md` and `.claude.json` and 266 still lists the unchanged `.claude/rules|commands|skills` outputs. 267 - `declaredOutputs("project")` and `declaredOutputs("")` both list `CLAUDE.md` 268 and `.mcp.json` (identical to today). 269 - The JSON pointer and section id are scope-invariant. 270 271 **Verification:** capability tests pass for all three scope values. 272 273 ### U3. Make the Claude adapter's emit paths scope-aware 274 275 **Goal:** `emitAgentsMD` and `emitMCPServerEntry` emit at the scope-resolved 276 paths so emitted ops stay inside the declared outputs (path-safety gate), and 277 resolve the sidecar coherently at user scope. 278 279 **Requirements:** R-US1, R-US2, R-US3, R-US6. 280 281 **Dependencies:** U1, U2. 282 283 **Files:** 284 - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit_tool_owned.go` — replace the 285 package-level `mcpJSONPath` / `claudeMDPath` / `claudeMDSidecar` literals 286 with scope-resolved values threaded from the captured scope (via `emitState` 287 or a path-resolver passed into `handleEmit`). 288 - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit.go` — `handleEmit` / `emitState` carry 289 the scope (or a resolved `paths` struct) so dispatch reaches the emitters. 290 - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/bundled.go` — `OnEmit` closure supplies the 291 captured scope. 292 - Update the package doc comment in `bundled.go:10-11` to note the 293 scope-dependent destinations. 294 - Tests: `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit_test.go`, 295 `emit_opcontent_test.go`. 296 297 **Approach:** Thread a resolved `paths{claudeMD, mcpJSON, sidecar}` struct 298 (computed once from scope) into `emitState`. The op *content*, JSON-pointer 299 locator, and markdown section markers are unchanged — only `Op.Path` differs. 300 Emitted paths must equal the U2 declared outputs so 301 `pathInDeclaredOutputs` passes. Sidecar at user scope: see OQ-1 — default to 302 **suppressing** the root `.agent-sync-managed` sidecar at user scope (do not 303 write `~/.agent-sync-managed`), pending the OQ-1 decision. 304 305 **Execution note:** Start with a characterization test asserting that at user 306 scope the emitted op paths are exactly the U2 declared outputs (this is the 307 gate-coupling invariant and the regression guard). 308 309 **Patterns to follow:** the current `emitMCPServerEntry` / `emitAgentsMD` 310 op construction; the cursor/codex adapters' single-path emit shape. 311 312 **Test scenarios:** 313 - At user scope: agents-md op path = `.claude/CLAUDE.md`; mcp op path = 314 `.claude.json`; both pass `pathInDeclaredOutputs` against U2's user-scope 315 declared outputs. 316 - At project scope: op paths are exactly `CLAUDE.md` / `.mcp.json` (unchanged), 317 sidecar still emitted at `.agent-sync-managed`. 318 - Body-validation guards (non-object MCP body, marker-injection in agents-md 319 body) still fire identically at both scopes. 320 - Sidecar: not emitted at root at user scope (per OQ-1 default). 321 322 **Verification:** emit tests green at both scopes; the emitted-equals-declared 323 invariant holds; `go test -race ./internal/adapter/...`. 324 325 ### U4. End-to-end `sync --user` coverage for Claude 326 327 **Goal:** Prove the fix at the real sync seam and lock it against regression. 328 329 **Requirements:** R-US1, R-US2, R-US3, R-US6. 330 331 **Dependencies:** U2, U3. 332 333 **Files:** 334 - Tests: `internal/cli/cmd_sync_test.go` (or a sibling) — a `--user` end-to-end 335 run using the hierarchy harness with an injected `Home` (the discovery 336 `Options.Home` override and `fsroot.OpenWorkspaceRoot` on that home). 337 338 **Approach:** Build a canonical source with one agents-md overlay node and one 339 mcp-server-entry, a user-home manifest with `targets: [claude]`, seed 340 `~/.claude.json` with a foreign top-level key and a foreign 341 `/mcpServers/other` entry, run `sync --user`, and assert: `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` 342 contains the managed section; `~/.claude.json` gained 343 `/mcpServers/agentsync_` while the foreign key and `/mcpServers/other` 344 are byte-intact; no `~/CLAUDE.md`, no `~/.mcp.json`, and (per OQ-1) no 345 `~/.agent-sync-managed` were written. Then re-run and assert idempotence. 346 Add a project-scope control asserting the legacy paths are still produced. 347 348 **Patterns to follow:** `internal/cli/cmd_sync_test.go` harness 349 (`makeCanonicalRepo`, the hierarchy/`--user` discovery override); the 350 tool-owned-entry merge tests in `internal/merge`. 351 352 **Test scenarios:** 353 - R-US1/R-US2: user-scope sync lands `.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `.claude.json` 354 merge; foreign `.claude.json` keys preserved. 355 - R-US3: a project-scope sync in the same test package still writes 356 `CLAUDE.md` / `.mcp.json`. 357 - Idempotent re-run reports unchanged and disturbs no foreign content. 358 359 **Verification:** end-to-end `--user` test passes; full `go test -race ./...` 360 green; coverage floor (80%) holds; `golangci-lint run` clean. 361 362 --- 363 364 ## Risks & Dependencies 365 366 - **Risk: corrupting `~/.claude.json` (elevated — confirmed against a real 367 file).** It is ~425 KB of whole-program Claude Code state: `oauthAccount`, 368 `projects`, history, ~95 top-level keys, and ~11 **pre-existing user MCP 369 servers** under `mcpServers` (none agent-sync's). `mcpServers` is one key 370 among many. A merge bug here corrupts the user's entire Claude Code config, 371 not a small workspace file. Mitigations: reuse the surgical JSON-pointer 372 tool-owned merge writing only `/mcpServers/agentsync_` (never a wholesale 373 write); U4 seeds a fixture with foreign top-level keys **and** foreign 374 sibling `mcpServers` entries and asserts all survive byte-intact; the 375 existing merge-layer tests. Strongly consider a pre-write size/parse sanity 376 check and never truncating on partial failure (atomic swap already covers 377 this — verify it holds for the user-scope target). 378 - **Risk: declared/emitted path drift.** If U2 and U3 disagree on the 379 user-scope paths, the path-safety gate (`runtime.go:500`) rejects the emit at 380 sync time. Mitigation: U3's emitted-equals-declared characterization test; 381 resolve both from one shared path-resolver. 382 - **Risk: scope not plumbed on every session path.** `capability.go` and 383 `target.go` both open sessions; missing the field on one yields silent 384 project-scope fallback at user scope. Mitigation: U1 updates both call sites; 385 a runtime test asserts the field is sent. 386 - **Dependency:** builds on multi-scope sync 387 (`docs/plans/2026-06-17-002-...`), which is what makes `--user` reach the 388 home root in the first place. No external dependency. 389 390 --- 391 392 ## Open Questions 393 394 - **OQ-1: sidecar at user scope.** The `.agent-sync-managed` strict-JSON 395 sidecar exists to advertise agent-sync ownership next to an 396 agent-sync-managed strict-JSON file (`.mcp.json`). At user scope the MCP 397 target is `~/.claude.json` — Claude's *own* shared config, not an 398 agent-sync-owned file — so writing `~/.agent-sync-managed` would over-claim. 399 **Proposed default: suppress the root sidecar at user scope.** Alternative: 400 relocate it under `~/.claude/` as a non-authoritative breadcrumb. Decide 401 before U3 lands. 402 - **OQ-2: wire field vs `_meta`.** This plan recommends a typed `Scope` field 403 on `InitializeParams`. If the team would rather not grow the typed handshake 404 surface, the alternative is the existing `Meta` raw field. The recommended 405 path is the typed field (schema-parity-testable, discoverable). 406 - **OQ-3 [RESOLVED 2026-06-18]: `.claude.json` uses the same `mcpServers` 407 key/shape as project `.mcp.json`.** Verified against a real 408 `~/.claude.json`: it has a top-level `mcpServers` object keyed by server 409 name, each entry `{command, args, type}` — identical to the project 410 `.mcp.json` shape. **Conclusion: user scope is a filename-only change 411 (`.mcp.json` → `.claude.json`); the JSON pointer stays 412 `/mcpServers/agentsync_`.** U2/U3 vary only `Op.Path` by scope, not the 413 locator. (See the reinforced corruption risk below — the real file is ~425 KB 414 of whole-program state with ~11 pre-existing user MCP servers.) 415 416 --- 417 418 ## Scope Boundaries 419 420 ### Deferred to follow-up work 421 - **Codex user scope.** Codex emits `AGENTS.md` at workspace root; at user 422 scope `~/AGENTS.md` may have the same class of problem. Out of scope here; 423 evaluate separately with the same scope-aware mechanism (U1 is reusable). 424 - **Cursor user scope.** Cursor outputs already live under `.cursor/`; confirm 425 no equivalent gap, but not part of this fix. 426 427 ### Out of scope 428 - Any change to `owned-subdir` / `shared-subdir` / `tool-owned-entry` merge 429 semantics. 430 - The user-scope memory's "use project scope until fixed" workaround guidance 431 (remove once this ships). 432 - A wire protocol version bump (the change is additive). 433

--- title: "fix: scope-aware Claude adapter output paths (sync --user targets ~/.claude)" status: draft date: 2026-06-18 type: fix origin: docs/plans/2026-06-17-002-feat-multi-scope-sync-plan.md master_plan: docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md --- # fix: scope-aware Claude adapter output paths (sync --user targets ~/.claude) ## Summary `sync --user` writes two Claude outputs to paths Claude Code never reads. The Claude adapter declares and emits **workspace-relative** paths (`CLAUDE.md`, `.mcp.json`) that are correct at project scope but wrong at user scope: at `--user` the scope root is the home directory (`internal/hierarchy/discover.go:108`, `Root = home`), so they resolve to `~/CLAUDE.md` and `~/.mcp.json`. Claude Code reads user-global config from `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and `~/.claude.json`. The files agent-sync writes at user scope are inert. The asymmetry is the tell: the adapter's other outputs (`.claude/rules/agent-sync`, `.claude/commands/agent-sync`, `.claude/skills`) already live under `.claude/`, so they land correctly at `~/.claude/...`. Only the two **root-level tool-owned-entry** outputs are misplaced — and `.mcp.json → ~/.claude.json` is a *filename* change, not a directory-prefix swap, so no generic prefix rewrite fixes it. This plan makes the Claude adapter **scope-aware**: the adapter learns the scope level (user / project / directory) at `initialize`, and at user scope declares + emits `.claude/CLAUDE.md` and `.claude.json` instead of the project-scope paths. The scope signal is threaded as an additive field through the existing handshake. Project and directory scope behavior is unchanged. --- ## Problem Frame **Confirmed dead-end write at user scope.** Verified by code trace (2026-06-18): - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/capabilities.go:73` `declaredOutputs()` returns static workspace-relative paths, including `{Path: "CLAUDE.md", Mode: ToolOwnedEntry, SectionID: "agent-sync"}` and `{Path: ".mcp.json", Mode: ToolOwnedEntry, JSONPointer: "/mcpServers"}`. - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit_tool_owned.go:12-15` hardcodes the same constants (`mcpJSONPath = ".mcp.json"`, `claudeMDPath = "CLAUDE.md"`, `claudeMDSidecar = ".agent-sync-managed"`); `emitMCPServerEntry` and `emitAgentsMD` emit ops at those literals. - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/bundled.go` `OnInitialize` **discards** its `InitializeParams` (`func(_, _ adapterkit.InitializeParams)`) and returns the static `declaredOutputs()` — so even though the handshake already carries `WorkspaceRoot`, the adapter ignores it. - `internal/cli/setup.go:107-116` builds one `engine.Request` per scope with `WorkspacePath: scopeRoot`; at user scope `scopeRoot = home` (`internal/hierarchy/discover.go:108`). `internal/engine/target.go:46` passes that straight into `adapter.SessionOptions.WorkspaceRoot`. - Claude Code reads user-global instructions from `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and user-scope MCP servers from `~/.claude.json` — neither of which the adapter targets. Net effect: `sync --user` succeeds, reports success, and writes `~/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.mcp.json` that Claude never loads. The path-safety gate already enforces the link that makes this fixable as a single coordinated change: `internal/adapter/runtime.go:500` `pathInDeclaredOutputs` accepts an emitted op only if its path is contained in a declared output. So declared outputs and emitted paths must move together — change one without the other and the gate rejects the emit. The existing in-code/YAML mirror invariant in `capabilities.go:34-36` is the same discipline. --- ## Requirements Trace - **R-US1** — At user scope, the Claude adapter declares and emits the agents-md overlay at `.claude/CLAUDE.md` (relative to the home root), and a `sync --user` writes the managed section there. → U2, U3, U4 - **R-US2** — At user scope, the Claude adapter declares and emits the mcp-server-entry at `.claude.json` (relative to the home root), merging into the `/mcpServers/agentsync_` pointer and preserving all unrelated keys in that file. → U2, U3, U4 - **R-US3** — At **project** and **directory** scope, declared outputs and emitted paths are byte-for-byte unchanged (`CLAUDE.md`, `.mcp.json`); no regression to today's behavior. → U2, U3, U4 - **R-US4** — The scope signal is an **additive, backward-compatible** extension to the initialize handshake: an adapter that ignores it defaults to project-scope paths; an absent signal is treated as project scope. → U1 - **R-US5** — The `.claude/...` owned-subdir / shared-subdir outputs (rules, commands, skills) are unchanged at every scope (they already land correctly under `~/.claude/`). → U2 - **R-US6** — The `.agent-sync-managed` strict-JSON sidecar behaves coherently at user scope (see Open Question OQ-1): it is **not** written next to `~/.claude.json` as if agent-sync owned that shared file. → U3 Success criteria: a `sync --user` for Claude writes `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and merges into `~/.claude.json` (preserving foreign keys); project/directory syncs are unchanged; `go test -race ./...`, `golangci-lint run`, and the 80% coverage floor stay green. --- ## High-Level Technical Design The adapter is the sole owner of Claude's on-disk path knowledge (the architecture's gateway/adapter boundary: core never encodes per-tool paths). So the fix lives in the adapter — the engine must only **tell the adapter which scope it is emitting for**. Today the handshake gives the adapter `WorkspaceRoot` but not the scope *level*; inferring "is this home?" from the root is fragile (symlinked homes, `--home` override in tests, nested repos at `$HOME`). We pass the level explicitly. ```mermaid flowchart TD A["hierarchy.Scope.Level
(user / project / directory)"] --> B["engine.Request.Scope
(new field)"] B --> C["adapter.SessionOptions.Scope"] C --> D["InitializeParams.Scope
(new additive field, omitempty)"] D --> E["claude OnInitialize:
capture scope into session state"] E --> F{scope == user?} F -->|yes| G["declaredOutputs(user):
.claude/CLAUDE.md + .claude.json"] F -->|no / absent| H["declaredOutputs(project):
CLAUDE.md + .mcp.json (unchanged)"] E --> I["claude OnEmit:
resolve paths from captured scope"] I --> F2{scope == user?} F2 -->|yes| J["emit agents-md -> .claude/CLAUDE.md
emit mcp-entry -> .claude.json
suppress root sidecar (OQ-1)"] F2 -->|no| K["emit -> CLAUDE.md / .mcp.json (unchanged)"] G -.path-safety gate.-> J H -.path-safety gate.-> K ``` **Key insight:** the bundled adapter's `run()` constructs a fresh `adapterkit.Server` per session, so `OnInitialize` and `OnEmit` can close over a single per-session `scope` variable. `declaredOutputs` and the emit helpers become functions of scope; everything else (tool-owned-entry merge machinery, JSON-pointer merge, markdown-section merge, the path-safety gate) is reused unchanged — only the *paths* the adapter chooses differ. --- ## Key Technical Decisions - **Scope is carried by an additive `Scope` field on the initialize handshake, not inferred from `WorkspaceRoot`.** Inference is unreliable (symlinked/overridden homes, a repo whose `.git` sits at `$HOME`). A first-class string enum (`"user"` | `"project"` | `"directory"`) on `InitializeParams` (contract + adapterkit, `omitempty`) is explicit, schema-parity-testable, and backward compatible: absent ⇒ project scope. This follows the project's "freeze the frame, grow capabilities" policy — the same additive shape used when `shared-subdir` was added (no version bump). *Alternative considered:* ride through the existing `InitializeParams.Meta` (`_meta`) raw field with no typed surface — rejected as less discoverable and untestable via schema parity. (See OQ-2.) - **The fix is in the adapter, not an engine-side path rewrite.** Having the engine remap `.mcp.json → .claude.json` / `CLAUDE.md → .claude/CLAUDE.md` for user scope would push per-tool, per-scope filename knowledge into core, violating the adapter-owns-its-tool's-paths boundary. The `.mcp.json → .claude.json` *filename* change (not a prefix move) makes a generic rewrite impossible anyway. - **`.claude.json` is merged surgically, never owned wholesale.** At user scope the MCP target is Claude's live combined config (`~/.claude.json` holds projects, history, auth, MRU state — far more than `mcpServers`). The existing `tool-owned-entry` JSON-pointer merge writes only `/mcpServers/agentsync_` and preserves every other key — exactly the right primitive. The risk is higher than `.mcp.json` (a bug corrupts a load-bearing file), so U4 includes a foreign-key-preservation test against a realistic `.claude.json` fixture. - **Default-to-project keeps all existing call sites correct.** The single-scope path (`prepareEngine`, used by `validate` and explicit `--workspace` sync) passes `LevelProject`; an absent wire field also means project. No existing test or behavior shifts unless `--user` is in play. - **Scope-awareness is a general adapter capability, applied here only to Claude.** The wire field is adapter-agnostic, but this plan changes only the Claude adapter. Cursor (`.cursor/...`) and Codex are out of scope (see Scope Boundaries — Codex's `AGENTS.md` at user scope is a separate question). --- ## Implementation Units ### U1. Thread a `Scope` signal through the initialize handshake **Goal:** Add an additive `Scope` field to the initialize params across the SDK type, the wire-contract mirror, and the JSON schema, and plumb it from the hierarchy scope level down to the adapter session. **Requirements:** R-US4. **Dependencies:** none. **Files:** - `pkg/adapterkit/types.go` — add `Scope string` to `InitializeParams` (`json:"scope,omitempty"`), with a doc comment enumerating `user`/`project`/`directory` and "absent ⇒ project". - `internal/adapter/contract/protocol.go` — mirror the field on the contract `InitializeParams` (after `IRVersion`, before `Meta`). - `internal/adapter/contract/schema/*initialize*.json` — add the optional `scope` property with the enum, if initialize params are schema-validated. - `internal/adapter/adapter.go` (`SessionOptions`) — add a `Scope` field. - `internal/adapter/runtime.go` `Initialize` — set `params.Scope` from `s.options.Scope` (default `"project"` when empty). - `internal/engine/request.go` — add `Scope string` (or `hierarchy.Level`) to `Request`; `internal/engine/target.go:46` and `internal/engine/capability.go:45` pass it into `SessionOptions`. - `internal/cli/setup.go` — `prepareScope` gains a scope-level parameter; the hierarchy orchestrator passes each `Scope.Level`, and `prepareEngine` passes `LevelProject`. Map `hierarchy.Level.String()` → the wire value. - Tests: `pkg/adapterkit/types_test.go`, `internal/adapter/contract/*_test.go`, the schema-parity test, and `internal/adapter/runtime_test.go`. **Approach:** Purely additive. The level already has a stable lowercase `String()` (`internal/hierarchy/hierarchy.go`: `user`/`project`/`directory`), so reuse it as the wire value rather than inventing a parallel enum. Confirm schema-parity (SDK ↔ contract ↔ JSON schema) holds with the new optional field, and that an initialize payload omitting `scope` still validates. **Patterns to follow:** the existing `IRVersion` / `ReservedPrefix` fields on `InitializeParams` and their round-trip + parity coverage; the `shared-subdir` additive-enum precedent in `docs/plans/2026-06-09-003-...`. **Test scenarios:** - `InitializeParams{Scope: "user"}` round-trips through marshal/unmarshal and validates against the schema. - An initialize payload with no `scope` key validates and decodes to `""` (runtime treats it as project). - Schema parity: SDK, contract, and JSON-schema agree on the field + enum. - `runtime.Initialize` sends `scope: "project"` when `SessionOptions.Scope` is empty (backward-compat default). **Verification:** parity + round-trip tests pass; the field exists in all three layers and is populated by the engine per scope. ### U2. Make the Claude adapter's declared outputs scope-aware **Goal:** `declaredOutputs` returns `.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `.claude.json` at user scope and the current `CLAUDE.md` + `.mcp.json` at project/directory scope; capture the scope in `OnInitialize`. **Requirements:** R-US1, R-US2, R-US3, R-US5. **Dependencies:** U1. **Files:** - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/bundled.go` — `OnInitialize` reads `params.Scope` and stores it in a per-session variable closed over by both handlers; pass scope into `declaredOutputs(scope)`. - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/capabilities.go` — `declaredOutputs` takes the scope and selects the two tool-owned-entry paths accordingly. The `.claude/rules/agent-sync`, `.claude/commands/agent-sync`, `.claude/skills`, and sidecar entries are unaffected at project scope; sidecar handling at user scope per U3/OQ-1. - Tests: `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/capabilities_test.go`. **Approach:** Introduce a small `scope` type (or reuse the wire string) and a `pathSet` helper that resolves the two tool-owned paths from scope. Keep the JSON pointer (`/mcpServers`) and section id (`agent-sync`) identical across scopes — only `Path` changes. Default/unknown scope ⇒ project paths. **Patterns to follow:** the current `declaredOutputs()` shape and the in-code/YAML mirror discipline (`capabilities.go:34`). **Test scenarios:** - `declaredOutputs("user")` lists `.claude/CLAUDE.md` and `.claude.json` and still lists the unchanged `.claude/rules|commands|skills` outputs. - `declaredOutputs("project")` and `declaredOutputs("")` both list `CLAUDE.md` and `.mcp.json` (identical to today). - The JSON pointer and section id are scope-invariant. **Verification:** capability tests pass for all three scope values. ### U3. Make the Claude adapter's emit paths scope-aware **Goal:** `emitAgentsMD` and `emitMCPServerEntry` emit at the scope-resolved paths so emitted ops stay inside the declared outputs (path-safety gate), and resolve the sidecar coherently at user scope. **Requirements:** R-US1, R-US2, R-US3, R-US6. **Dependencies:** U1, U2. **Files:** - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit_tool_owned.go` — replace the package-level `mcpJSONPath` / `claudeMDPath` / `claudeMDSidecar` literals with scope-resolved values threaded from the captured scope (via `emitState` or a path-resolver passed into `handleEmit`). - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit.go` — `handleEmit` / `emitState` carry the scope (or a resolved `paths` struct) so dispatch reaches the emitters. - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/bundled.go` — `OnEmit` closure supplies the captured scope. - Update the package doc comment in `bundled.go:10-11` to note the scope-dependent destinations. - Tests: `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit_test.go`, `emit_opcontent_test.go`. **Approach:** Thread a resolved `paths{claudeMD, mcpJSON, sidecar}` struct (computed once from scope) into `emitState`. The op *content*, JSON-pointer locator, and markdown section markers are unchanged — only `Op.Path` differs. Emitted paths must equal the U2 declared outputs so `pathInDeclaredOutputs` passes. Sidecar at user scope: see OQ-1 — default to **suppressing** the root `.agent-sync-managed` sidecar at user scope (do not write `~/.agent-sync-managed`), pending the OQ-1 decision. **Execution note:** Start with a characterization test asserting that at user scope the emitted op paths are exactly the U2 declared outputs (this is the gate-coupling invariant and the regression guard). **Patterns to follow:** the current `emitMCPServerEntry` / `emitAgentsMD` op construction; the cursor/codex adapters' single-path emit shape. **Test scenarios:** - At user scope: agents-md op path = `.claude/CLAUDE.md`; mcp op path = `.claude.json`; both pass `pathInDeclaredOutputs` against U2's user-scope declared outputs. - At project scope: op paths are exactly `CLAUDE.md` / `.mcp.json` (unchanged), sidecar still emitted at `.agent-sync-managed`. - Body-validation guards (non-object MCP body, marker-injection in agents-md body) still fire identically at both scopes. - Sidecar: not emitted at root at user scope (per OQ-1 default). **Verification:** emit tests green at both scopes; the emitted-equals-declared invariant holds; `go test -race ./internal/adapter/...`. ### U4. End-to-end `sync --user` coverage for Claude **Goal:** Prove the fix at the real sync seam and lock it against regression. **Requirements:** R-US1, R-US2, R-US3, R-US6. **Dependencies:** U2, U3. **Files:** - Tests: `internal/cli/cmd_sync_test.go` (or a sibling) — a `--user` end-to-end run using the hierarchy harness with an injected `Home` (the discovery `Options.Home` override and `fsroot.OpenWorkspaceRoot` on that home). **Approach:** Build a canonical source with one agents-md overlay node and one mcp-server-entry, a user-home manifest with `targets: [claude]`, seed `~/.claude.json` with a foreign top-level key and a foreign `/mcpServers/other` entry, run `sync --user`, and assert: `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` contains the managed section; `~/.claude.json` gained `/mcpServers/agentsync_` while the foreign key and `/mcpServers/other` are byte-intact; no `~/CLAUDE.md`, no `~/.mcp.json`, and (per OQ-1) no `~/.agent-sync-managed` were written. Then re-run and assert idempotence. Add a project-scope control asserting the legacy paths are still produced. **Patterns to follow:** `internal/cli/cmd_sync_test.go` harness (`makeCanonicalRepo`, the hierarchy/`--user` discovery override); the tool-owned-entry merge tests in `internal/merge`. **Test scenarios:** - R-US1/R-US2: user-scope sync lands `.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `.claude.json` merge; foreign `.claude.json` keys preserved. - R-US3: a project-scope sync in the same test package still writes `CLAUDE.md` / `.mcp.json`. - Idempotent re-run reports unchanged and disturbs no foreign content. **Verification:** end-to-end `--user` test passes; full `go test -race ./...` green; coverage floor (80%) holds; `golangci-lint run` clean. --- ## Risks & Dependencies - **Risk: corrupting `~/.claude.json` (elevated — confirmed against a real file).** It is ~425 KB of whole-program Claude Code state: `oauthAccount`, `projects`, history, ~95 top-level keys, and ~11 **pre-existing user MCP servers** under `mcpServers` (none agent-sync's). `mcpServers` is one key among many. A merge bug here corrupts the user's entire Claude Code config, not a small workspace file. Mitigations: reuse the surgical JSON-pointer tool-owned merge writing only `/mcpServers/agentsync_` (never a wholesale write); U4 seeds a fixture with foreign top-level keys **and** foreign sibling `mcpServers` entries and asserts all survive byte-intact; the existing merge-layer tests. Strongly consider a pre-write size/parse sanity check and never truncating on partial failure (atomic swap already covers this — verify it holds for the user-scope target). - **Risk: declared/emitted path drift.** If U2 and U3 disagree on the user-scope paths, the path-safety gate (`runtime.go:500`) rejects the emit at sync time. Mitigation: U3's emitted-equals-declared characterization test; resolve both from one shared path-resolver. - **Risk: scope not plumbed on every session path.** `capability.go` and `target.go` both open sessions; missing the field on one yields silent project-scope fallback at user scope. Mitigation: U1 updates both call sites; a runtime test asserts the field is sent. - **Dependency:** builds on multi-scope sync (`docs/plans/2026-06-17-002-...`), which is what makes `--user` reach the home root in the first place. No external dependency. --- ## Open Questions - **OQ-1: sidecar at user scope.** The `.agent-sync-managed` strict-JSON sidecar exists to advertise agent-sync ownership next to an agent-sync-managed strict-JSON file (`.mcp.json`). At user scope the MCP target is `~/.claude.json` — Claude's *own* shared config, not an agent-sync-owned file — so writing `~/.agent-sync-managed` would over-claim. **Proposed default: suppress the root sidecar at user scope.** Alternative: relocate it under `~/.claude/` as a non-authoritative breadcrumb. Decide before U3 lands. - **OQ-2: wire field vs `_meta`.** This plan recommends a typed `Scope` field on `InitializeParams`. If the team would rather not grow the typed handshake surface, the alternative is the existing `Meta` raw field. The recommended path is the typed field (schema-parity-testable, discoverable). - **OQ-3 [RESOLVED 2026-06-18]: `.claude.json` uses the same `mcpServers` key/shape as project `.mcp.json`.** Verified against a real `~/.claude.json`: it has a top-level `mcpServers` object keyed by server name, each entry `{command, args, type}` — identical to the project `.mcp.json` shape. **Conclusion: user scope is a filename-only change (`.mcp.json` → `.claude.json`); the JSON pointer stays `/mcpServers/agentsync_`.** U2/U3 vary only `Op.Path` by scope, not the locator. (See the reinforced corruption risk below — the real file is ~425 KB of whole-program state with ~11 pre-existing user MCP servers.) --- ## Scope Boundaries ### Deferred to follow-up work - **Codex user scope.** Codex emits `AGENTS.md` at workspace root; at user scope `~/AGENTS.md` may have the same class of problem. Out of scope here; evaluate separately with the same scope-aware mechanism (U1 is reusable). - **Cursor user scope.** Cursor outputs already live under `.cursor/`; confirm no equivalent gap, but not part of this fix. ### Out of scope - Any change to `owned-subdir` / `shared-subdir` / `tool-owned-entry` merge semantics. - The user-scope memory's "use project scope until fixed" workaround guidance (remove once this ships). - A wire protocol version bump (the change is additive).

content.text:
A thorough draft plan for this exact fix **already exists** — `docs/plans/2026-06-18-001-fix-claude-user-scope-paths-plan.md` (dated today, status `draft`). It's solid: full problem trace, requirements (R-US1–6), the threading design I just re-derived (additive `Scope` field → adapter branches paths), 4 TDD units (U1–U4), and risk analysis (notably: `~/.claude.json` is ~425 KB of whole-program state with ~11 existing MCP servers — merge must be surgical).

It's blocked only by **two open questions**. The plan makes a clear recommendation on each; I agree with both. Let me get your call so I can flip it to `approved` and implement:

questions.question: OQ-1: What happens to the `.agent-sync-managed` strict-JSON sidecar at user scope? (At user scope the MCP target is `~/.claude.json` — Claude's own shared config, not an agent-sync-owned file.)

options.label: Suppress at user scope (plan's rec)
options.description: Don't write ~/.agent-sync-managed. The sidecar advertises agent-sync ownership of a strict-JSON file, but ~/.claude.json is Claude's own — writing the marker would over-claim. Recommended in the plan.

options.label: Relocate under ~/.claude/
options.description: Write it as a non-authoritative breadcrumb under ~/.claude/ instead of the home root. Keeps a marker without sitting next to ~/.claude.json.

questions.question: OQ-2: How is the scope signal carried on the initialize handshake?

options.label: Typed Scope field (plan's rec)
options.description: Add `Scope string` (json:"scope,omitempty") to InitializeParams across SDK + contract + JSON schema. Matches existing IRVersion/ReservedPrefix pattern, schema-parity-testable, additive (no version bump). Recommended.

options.label: Ride through _meta
options.description: Pass scope through the existing InitializeParams.Meta raw field with no typed surface. Less discoverable, not schema-parity-testable.

content.text: Now record the OQ resolutions in the plan:

input.old_string:
- **OQ-1: sidecar at user scope.** The `.agent-sync-managed` strict-JSON
  sidecar exists to advertise agent-sync ownership next to an
  agent-sync-managed strict-JSON file (`.mcp.json`). At user scope the MCP
  target is `~/.claude.json` — Claude's *own* shared config, not an
  agent-sync-owned file — so writing `~/.agent-sync-managed` would over-claim.
  **Proposed default: suppress the root sidecar at user scope.** Alternative:
  relocate it under `~/.claude/` as a non-authoritative breadcrumb. Decide
  before U3 lands.
- **OQ-2: wire field vs `_meta`.** This plan recommends a typed `Scope` field
  on `InitializeParams`. If the team would rather not grow the typed handshake
  surface, the alternative is the existing `Meta` raw field. The recommended
  path is the typed field (schema-parity-testable, discoverable).
input.new_string:
- **OQ-1 [RESOLVED 2026-06-18]: suppress the sidecar at user scope.** The
  `.agent-sync-managed` strict-JSON sidecar exists to advertise agent-sync
  ownership next to an agent-sync-managed strict-JSON file (`.mcp.json`). At
  user scope the MCP target is `~/.claude.json` — Claude's *own* shared config,
  not an agent-sync-owned file — so writing `~/.agent-sync-managed` would
  over-claim. **Decision: at user scope the Claude adapter does not declare or
  emit the `.agent-sync-managed` sidecar.** U2 omits it from user-scope declared
  outputs; U3 suppresses the emit; U4 asserts no `~/.agent-sync-managed`.
- **OQ-2 [RESOLVED 2026-06-18]: typed `Scope` field on `InitializeParams`.**
  Carried as `Scope string` (`json:"scope,omitempty"`) across SDK + contract +
  JSON schema, mirroring the existing `IRVersion`/`ReservedPrefix` fields.
  Schema-parity-testable, discoverable, additive (absent ⇒ project; no version
  bump). The `_meta` alternative is rejected.

content.content:
1	// Package claude implements the bundled agent-sync adapter for Claude Code.
2	//
3	// The adapter consumes IR v1 nodes and emits the v1 op vocabulary
4	// (write_file, write_tool_owned, mkdir, delete, warning) for Claude
5	// Code's actual on-disk layout:
6	//
7	//   - rule    -> .claude/rules/agent-sync/<id>.md
8	//   - command -> .claude/commands/agent-sync/<id>.md
9	//   - skill   -> .claude/skills/agent-sync-<id>/SKILL.md plus assets
10	//   - mcp-server-entry -> .mcp.json (workspace root) via write_tool_owned
11	//   - agents-md (companion overlay) -> CLAUDE.md (workspace root)
12	//   - plugin-reference -> warning, unsupported by design
13	//
14	// Bundled() returns an *adapter.BundledAdapter ready for the runtime
15	// to spawn over an in-process pipe; nothing else in this package is
16	// part of the public surface.
17	package claude
18	
19	import (
20		"context"
21		"fmt"
22		"io"
23	
24		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/adapter"
25		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/pkg/adapterkit"
26	)
27	
28	const (
29		adapterName    = "claude"
30		adapterVersion = "0.1"
31		reservedPrefix = ".claude"
32	)
33	
34	// Bundled returns the adapter.BundledAdapter for the claude target.
35	// The runtime registers it via adapter.DiscoverOptions.Bundled.
36	func Bundled() *adapter.BundledAdapter {
37		return &adapter.BundledAdapter{
38			Manifest: adapter.AdapterManifest{
39				Name:            adapterName,
40				Version:         adapterVersion,
41				ContractVersion: adapter.ContractVersionV1,
42				ReservedPrefix:  reservedPrefix,
43				// Bundled adapters are spawned in-process via Run; the
44				// Command slice is required by the shared manifest
45				// validator (validateAdapterManifest in
46				// internal/adapter/discover.go) but never executed for
47				// SourceBundled — the runtime takes the Run path. The
48				// placeholder name is recorded for diagnostics only.
49				Command: []string{"agent-sync-adapter-claude-bundled"},
50			},
51			Run: run,
52		}
53	}
54	
55	// run is the in-process entry point invoked by InprocTransport. It
56	// constructs an adapterkit.Server bound to the supplied pipes, wires
57	// up the lifecycle handlers, and serves until context is cancelled
58	// or the runtime sends shutdown.
59	func run(ctx context.Context, stdin io.Reader, stdout io.Writer) error {
60		server := adapterkit.NewServer(adapterkit.ServerOptions{
61			Name:    adapterName,
62			Version: adapterVersion,
63			Stdin:   stdin,
64			Stdout:  stdout,
65			// stderr defaults to os.Stderr; bundled in-process adapters
66			// share the parent process so this routes diagnostics to the
67			// CLI's own stderr, which is the documented place for them.
68			// Getenv defaults to os.Getenv; bundled adapters skip the
69			// magic-cookie check (the runtime sets cookie="" for bundled,
70			// see internal/adapter/runtime.go Initialize), so the env-var
71			// read still happens but the runtime ignores any echoed
72			// cookie value.
73			//
74			// We supply a constant 32-hex string for the env-var so the
75			// SDK's startup magic-cookie format check passes; the runtime
76			// ignores the echoed value for bundled adapters.
77			Getenv: bundledGetenv,
78		})
79	
80		server.OnInitialize(func(_ context.Context, _ adapterkit.InitializeParams) (adapterkit.InitializeResult, error) {
81			return adapterkit.InitializeResult{
82				Capabilities:    capabilitiesForWire(),
83				DeclaredOutputs: declaredOutputs(),
84			}, nil
85		})
86	
87		server.OnEmit(handleEmit)
88	
89		if err := server.Run(ctx); err != nil {
90			return fmt.Errorf("claude bundled adapter: %w", err)
91		}
92		return nil
93	}
94	
95	// bundledCookie is a 32-hex placeholder sent through the adapterkit
96	// Server's cookie format check. Bundled adapters skip cookie
97	// validation at the runtime layer (see internal/adapter/runtime.go
98	// Initialize: cookie validation is gated on Source != SourceBundled),
99	// so the value is opaque to the runtime — only the SDK's "format
100	// must be 32 hex" check observes it.
101	const bundledCookie = "00000000000000000000000000000000"
102	
103	func bundledGetenv(name string) string {
104		if name == adapterkit.CookieEnvVar {
105			return bundledCookie
106		}
107		return ""
108	}
109	
110	// handleEmit lives in emit.go.
111

Now the emit-paths file (the constants and the two tool-owned emitters + sidecar):

content.content:
1	package claude
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7	
8		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/pkg/adapterkit"
9	)
10	
11	const (
12		mcpJSONPath        = ".mcp.json"
13		mcpJSONPointerBase = "/mcpServers/agentsync_"
14		claudeMDPath       = "CLAUDE.md"
15		claudeMDSidecar    = ".agent-sync-managed"
16		sectionIDPrefix    = "agent-sync:"
17	)
18	
19	// markerOpenBytes is the literal HTML-comment opener every agent-sync
20	// section marker uses. Body content for an agents-md node is rejected
21	// when it contains this byte sequence: a hostile body could otherwise
22	// inject a fake end-marker followed by a fake begin-marker, splitting
23	// the managed section in CLAUDE.md and confusing the merge step.
24	var markerOpenBytes = []byte("<!-- agent-sync:")
25	
26	// emitMCPServerEntry emits one write_tool_owned op into .mcp.json
27	// at /mcpServers/agentsync_<id>, plus a sidecar .agent-sync-managed marker
28	// (deduplicated per emit) that advertises ownership next to the
29	// strict-JSON file.
30	//
31	// The body is required to parse as a JSON object. A scalar, array,
32	// or boolean body would corrupt the merged .mcp.json: Claude Code
33	// expects /mcpServers/<key> to map to an object with command/args
34	// fields, and storing a non-object value silently breaks every MCP
35	// load for the workspace.
36	//
37	// Validation is two-stage:
38	//  1. json.Valid — proves the bytes parse, catching string-encoded
39	//     bodies like "{" that look object-shaped at the first byte but
40	//     are actually unterminated JSON.
41	//  2. isJSONObject — proves the top-level structure is `{...}` and
42	//     not a number/array/bool/null that json.Valid alone would
43	//     accept.
44	func emitMCPServerEntry(emitted *emittedOps, node irNode, state *emitState) error {
45		body, err := decodeBodyOrPassthrough(node.Body)
46		if err != nil {
47			return wrapBodyErr(node, err)
48		}
49		if !json.Valid(body) {
50			return &adapterkit.Error{
51				Code:    adapterkit.CodeInvalidParams,
52				Message: fmt.Sprintf("claude: mcp-server-entry %q body is not valid JSON; refusing to corrupt .mcp.json", node.ID),
53			}
54		}
55		if !isJSONObject(body) {
56			return &adapterkit.Error{
57				Code:    adapterkit.CodeInvalidParams,
58				Message: fmt.Sprintf("claude: mcp-server-entry %q body must be a JSON object (got non-object)", node.ID),
59			}
60		}
61	
62		emitted.add(adapterkit.OpWriteToolOwned{
63			Path:    mcpJSONPath,
64			Kind:    adapterkit.ToolOwnedKindJSONPointer,
65			Locator: mcpJSONPointerBase + node.ID,
66			Content: body,
67		})
68	
69		if !state.sidecarEmitted {
70			state.sidecarEmitted = true
71			sidecar, err := adapterkit.NewOpWriteFile(claudeMDSidecar, 0o644, jsonSidecarMarker())
72			if err != nil {
73				return wrapOpErr(node, err)
74			}
75			emitted.add(sidecar)
76		}
77		return nil
78	}
79	
80	// emitAgentsMD writes the agents-md companion overlay into
81	// CLAUDE.md (workspace root) as a managed section between
82	// <!-- agent-sync:begin id=<id> --> and <!-- agent-sync:end id=<id> -->
83	// markers. CLAUDE.md is tool-owned by Claude Code; user content
84	// outside the managed section is preserved by the merge step
85	// (Unit 12a).
86	//
87	// The engine owns the begin/end markers — it renders the managed block
88	// from the locator during the markdown-section merge. The adapter passes
89	// the INNER body only; sending marker-wrapped content is rejected by the
90	// merge ("the engine owns markers; pass inner body only"). This mirrors
91	// the cursor and codex adapters.
92	//
93	// The body is still rejected when it contains the agent-sync marker
94	// opener. Without this guard a body containing
95	// `<!-- agent-sync:end id=other -->` could forge another section,
96	// leaving the merged CLAUDE.md with conflicting markers the merge step
97	// has no way to resolve safely.
98	//
99	// No managed-file header is added inside the section: the begin/end
100	// markers serve as the equivalent ownership advertisement, and a
101	// header inside a user-owned markdown file would be visually noisy.
102	func emitAgentsMD(emitted *emittedOps, node irNode) error {
103		body, err := decodeBodyOrPassthrough(node.Body)
104		if err != nil {
105			return wrapBodyErr(node, err)
106		}
107		if bytes.Contains(body, markerOpenBytes) {
108			return &adapterkit.Error{
109				Code:    adapterkit.CodeInvalidParams,
110				Message: fmt.Sprintf("claude: agents-md %q body contains agent-sync marker syntax (%q); refusing to corrupt CLAUDE.md", node.ID, string(markerOpenBytes)),
111			}
112		}
113	
114		emitted.add(adapterkit.OpWriteToolOwned{
115			Path:    claudeMDPath,
116			Kind:    adapterkit.ToolOwnedKindMarkdownSection,
117			Locator: sectionIDPrefix + node.ID,
118			Content: body,
119		})
120		return nil
121	}
122	
123	// emitPluginReferenceWarning surfaces the unsupported declaration
124	// for a plugin-reference node. Claude Code does not load
125	// project-level plugin registries, so the adapter declines and
126	// surfaces a degradation warning the sync engine will report.
127	//
128	// No write_file is emitted. The runtime's capability-lied check
129	// (internal/adapter/runtime.go) only fires for kinds the adapter
130	// declared as `supported`, so emitting a warning-only result here
131	// is safe — plugin-reference is declared `unsupported` in
132	// capabilities.yaml.
133	func emitPluginReferenceWarning(emitted *emittedOps, node irNode) error {
134		emitted.add(adapterkit.OpWarning{
135			ConceptID: node.ID,
136			Status:    adapterkit.WarningStatusDegraded,
137			Note:      "Claude Code does not load project-level plugin-reference registries; install plugins via Claude Code's own plugin command",
138		})
139		return nil
140	}
141	
142	// isJSONObject reports whether body is a JSON object (`{...}`),
143	// not a scalar, array, or null. Assumes the caller already verified
144	// the bytes are valid JSON via decodeBodyOrPassthrough; the check
145	// here is purely structural — first non-whitespace byte must be `{`.
146	func isJSONObject(body []byte) bool {
147		for _, b := range body {
148			switch b {
149			case ' ', '\t', '\n', '\r':
150				continue
151			case '{':
152				return true
153			default:
154				return false
155			}
156		}
157		return false
158	}
159

1 --- 2 title: "fix: scope-aware Claude adapter output paths (sync --user targets ~/.claude)" 3 status: approved 4 date: 2026-06-18 5 type: fix 6 origin: docs/plans/2026-06-17-002-feat-multi-scope-sync-plan.md 7 master_plan: docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md 8 --- 9 10 # fix: scope-aware Claude adapter output paths (sync --user targets ~/.claude) 11 12 ## Summary 13 14 `sync --user` writes two Claude outputs to paths Claude Code never reads. 15 The Claude adapter declares and emits **workspace-relative** paths 16 (`CLAUDE.md`, `.mcp.json`) that are correct at project scope but wrong at 17 user scope: at `--user` the scope root is the home directory 18 (`internal/hierarchy/discover.go:108`, `Root = home`), so they resolve to 19 `~/CLAUDE.md` and `~/.mcp.json`. Claude Code reads user-global config from 20 `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and `~/.claude.json`. The files agent-sync writes at 21 user scope are inert. 22 23 The asymmetry is the tell: the adapter's other outputs 24 (`.claude/rules/agent-sync`, `.claude/commands/agent-sync`, `.claude/skills`) 25 already live under `.claude/`, so they land correctly at `~/.claude/...`. 26 Only the two **root-level tool-owned-entry** outputs are misplaced — and 27 `.mcp.json → ~/.claude.json` is a *filename* change, not a directory-prefix 28 swap, so no generic prefix rewrite fixes it. 29 30 This plan makes the Claude adapter **scope-aware**: the adapter learns the 31 scope level (user / project / directory) at `initialize`, and at user scope 32 declares + emits `.claude/CLAUDE.md` and `.claude.json` instead of the 33 project-scope paths. The scope signal is threaded as an additive field 34 through the existing handshake. Project and directory scope behavior is 35 unchanged. 36 37 --- 38 39 ## Problem Frame 40 41 **Confirmed dead-end write at user scope.** Verified by code trace 42 (2026-06-18): 43 44 - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/capabilities.go:73` `declaredOutputs()` 45 returns static workspace-relative paths, including 46 `{Path: "CLAUDE.md", Mode: ToolOwnedEntry, SectionID: "agent-sync"}` and 47 `{Path: ".mcp.json", Mode: ToolOwnedEntry, JSONPointer: "/mcpServers"}`. 48 - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit_tool_owned.go:12-15` hardcodes the 49 same constants (`mcpJSONPath = ".mcp.json"`, `claudeMDPath = "CLAUDE.md"`, 50 `claudeMDSidecar = ".agent-sync-managed"`); `emitMCPServerEntry` and 51 `emitAgentsMD` emit ops at those literals. 52 - `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/bundled.go` `OnInitialize` **discards** 53 its `InitializeParams` (`func(_, _ adapterkit.InitializeParams)`) and 54 returns the static `declaredOutputs()` — so even though the handshake 55 already carries `WorkspaceRoot`, the adapter ignores it. 56 - `internal/cli/setup.go:107-116` builds one `engine.Request` per scope with 57 `WorkspacePath: scopeRoot`; at user scope `scopeRoot = home` 58 (`internal/hierarchy/discover.go:108`). `internal/engine/target.go:46` 59 passes that straight into `adapter.SessionOptions.WorkspaceRoot`. 60 - Claude Code reads user-global instructions from `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and 61 user-scope MCP servers from `~/.claude.json` — neither of which the 62 adapter targets. 63 64 Net effect: `sync --user` succeeds, reports success, and writes 65 `~/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.mcp.json` that Claude never loads. 66 67 The path-safety gate already enforces the link that makes this fixable as a 68 single coordinated change: `internal/adapter/runtime.go:500` 69 `pathInDeclaredOutputs` accepts an emitted op only if its path is contained 70 in a declared output. So declared outputs and emitted paths must move 71 together — change one without the other and the gate rejects the emit. The 72 existing in-code/YAML mirror invariant in `capabilities.go:34-36` is the same 73 discipline. 74 75 --- 76 77 ## Requirements Trace 78 79 - **R-US1** — At user scope, the Claude adapter declares and emits the 80 agents-md overlay at `.claude/CLAUDE.md` (relative to the home root), and a 81 `sync --user` writes the managed section there. → U2, U3, U4 82 - **R-US2** — At user scope, the Claude adapter declares and emits the 83 mcp-server-entry at `.claude.json` (relative to the home root), merging into 84 the `/mcpServers/agentsync_` pointer and preserving all unrelated keys 85 in that file. → U2, U3, U4 86 - **R-US3** — At **project** and **directory** scope, declared outputs and 87 emitted paths are byte-for-byte unchanged (`CLAUDE.md`, `.mcp.json`); no 88 regression to today's behavior. → U2, U3, U4 89 - **R-US4** — The scope signal is an **additive, backward-compatible** 90 extension to the initialize handshake: an adapter that ignores it defaults 91 to project-scope paths; an absent signal is treated as project scope. → U1 92 - **R-US5** — The `.claude/...` owned-subdir / shared-subdir outputs 93 (rules, commands, skills) are unchanged at every scope (they already land 94 correctly under `~/.claude/`). → U2 95 - **R-US6** — The `.agent-sync-managed` strict-JSON sidecar behaves 96 coherently at user scope (see Open Question OQ-1): it is **not** written 97 next to `~/.claude.json` as if agent-sync owned that shared file. → U3 98 99 Success criteria: a `sync --user` for Claude writes `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and 100 merges into `~/.claude.json` (preserving foreign keys); project/directory 101 syncs are unchanged; `go test -race ./...`, `golangci-lint run`, and the 80% 102 coverage floor stay green. 103 104 --- 105 106 ## High-Level Technical Design 107 108 The adapter is the sole owner of Claude's on-disk path knowledge (the 109 architecture's gateway/adapter boundary: core never encodes per-tool paths). 110 So the fix lives in the adapter — the engine must only **tell the adapter 111 which scope it is emitting for**. Today the handshake gives the adapter 112 `WorkspaceRoot` but not the scope *level*; inferring "is this home?" from the 113 root is fragile (symlinked homes, `--home` override in tests, nested repos at 114 `$HOME`). We pass the level explicitly. 115 116 ```mermaid 117 flowchart TD 118 A["hierarchy.Scope.Level
(user / project / directory)"] --> B["engine.Request.Scope
(new field)"] 119 B --> C["adapter.SessionOptions.Scope"] 120 C --> D["InitializeParams.Scope
(new additive field, omitempty)"] 121 D --> E["claude OnInitialize:
capture scope into session state"] 122 E --> F{scope == user?} 123 F -->|yes| G["declaredOutputs(user):
.claude/CLAUDE.md + .claude.json"] 124 F -->|no / absent| H["declaredOutputs(project):
CLAUDE.md + .mcp.json (unchanged)"] 125 E --> I["claude OnEmit:
resolve paths from captured scope"] 126 I --> F2{scope == user?} 127 F2 -->|yes| J["emit agents-md -> .claude/CLAUDE.md
emit mcp-entry -> .claude.json

A 147 first-class string enum (`"user"` | `"project"` | `"directory"`) on 148 `InitializeParams` (contract + adapterkit, `omitempty`) is explicit, 149 schema-parity-testable, and backward compatible: absent ⇒ project scope. 150 This follows the project's "freeze the frame, grow capabilities" policy — 151 the same additive shape used when `shared-subdir` was added (no version 152 bump). *Alternative considered:* ride through the existing ... content.content:
1	package cli
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"context"
6		"encoding/json"
7		"errors"
8		"io"
9		"log/slog"
10		"os"
11		"path/filepath"
12		"testing"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/coverage"
16		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/engine"
17		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/hierarchy"
18		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/ir"
19		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/report"
20		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/trust"
21	)
22	
23	// hierarchyLocalDirManifest is the in-repo (local_dir) manifest used by the hierarchy
24	// tests: no git, no network, no pin.
25	const hierarchyLocalDirManifest = "version: 1\n" +
26		"canonical:\n" +
27		"  local_dir: .agents\n" +
28		"targets:\n" +
29		"  - claude\n"
30	
31	// hierarchyTree builds a home/repo/packages/api tree with a project manifest
32	// at the repo root and a directory manifest in packages/api, each with one
33	// authored skill. The repo root carries a .git dir so it is the project root.
34	// Returns (home, repoRoot, nestedDir).
35	func hierarchyTree(t *testing.T) (string, string, string) {
36		t.Helper()
37		home := t.TempDir()
38		repo := filepath.Join(home, "repo")
39		nested := filepath.Join(repo, "packages", "api")
40		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(repo, ".git"), 0o755); err != nil {
41			t.Fatal(err)
42		}
43		if err := os.MkdirAll(nested, 0o755); err != nil {
44			t.Fatal(err)
45		}
46		// Project scope.
47		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(repo, ".agent-sync.yaml"), []byte(hierarchyLocalDirManifest), 0o644); err != nil {
48			t.Fatal(err)
49		}
50		writeWS(t, repo, ".agents/skills/proj-skill/SKILL.md", "project skill body\n")
51		// Directory scope.
52		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(nested, ".agent-sync.yaml"), []byte(hierarchyLocalDirManifest), 0o644); err != nil {
53			t.Fatal(err)
54		}
55		writeWS(t, nested, ".agents/skills/api-skill/SKILL.md", "api skill body\n")
56		return home, repo, nested
57	}
58	
59	// newTestRuntime builds a runtimeContext suitable for driving prepareScope /
60	// runHierarchySync directly, mirroring how the root command populates rc.
61	func newTestRuntime() *runtimeContext {
62		return &runtimeContext{
63			Access: Access{NonInteractive: true},
64			Logger: slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(io.Discard, nil)),
65			Flags:  PersistentFlags{NonInteractive: true},
66		}
67	}
68	
69	func TestRunHierarchySyncEmitsEachScope(t *testing.T) {
70		home, repo, nested := hierarchyTree(t)
71		rc := newTestRuntime()
72		now := time.Date(2026, 6, 17, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
73	
74		outcomes, err := runHierarchySync(
75			context.Background(), rc, nested, home,
76			hierarchySyncOptions{IncludeUser: false, EngineOpts: engine.Options{
77				Mode:   report.ModeAtomic,
78				Now:    func() time.Time { return now },
79				Logger: rc.Logger,
80			}},
81			now,
82		)
83		if err != nil {
84			t.Fatalf("runHierarchySync: %v", err)
85		}
86	
87		if len(outcomes) != 2 {
88			t.Fatalf("got %d outcomes, want 2", len(outcomes))
89		}
90		if outcomes[0].Scope.Level != hierarchy.LevelProject {
91			t.Errorf("first scope level = %s, want project", outcomes[0].Scope.Level)
92		}
93		if outcomes[1].Scope.Level != hierarchy.LevelDirectory {
94			t.Errorf("second scope level = %s, want directory", outcomes[1].Scope.Level)
95		}
96		for _, o := range outcomes {
97			if o.Err != nil {
98				t.Fatalf("scope %s errored: %v", o.Scope.Root, o.Err)
99			}
100		}
101	
102		// Project scope emitted under repo/.claude.
103		mustExist(t, filepath.Join(repo, ".claude", "skills", "agent-sync-proj-skill", "SKILL.md"))
104		// Directory scope emitted under packages/api/.claude.
105		mustExist(t, filepath.Join(nested, ".claude", "skills", "agent-sync-api-skill", "SKILL.md"))
106		// The user scope was NOT emitted: no .claude under home itself.
107		mustNotExist(t, filepath.Join(home, ".claude"))
108	
109		if got := hierarchyExitCode(outcomes); got != 0 {
110			t.Errorf("aggregate exit = %d, want 0", got)
111		}
112	}
113	
114	// runSyncHierarchy drives the real sync cobra command on the hierarchy path:
115	// it sets cwd (so discovery walks from there), swaps the home seam to keep the
116	// suite hermetic, and deliberately omits --workspace so discovery runs.
117	func runSyncHierarchy(t *testing.T, cwd, home string, extraArgs ...string) (string, string, error) {
118		t.Helper()
119		t.Chdir(cwd)
120		prev := resolveHome
121		resolveHome = func() (string, error) { return home, nil }
122		t.Cleanup(func() { resolveHome = prev })
123	
124		var out, errBuf bytes.Buffer
125		root := NewRootCommand(RootDeps{Out: &out, Err: &errBuf, Version: "test"})
126		args := append([]string{"sync", "--non-interactive"}, extraArgs...)
127		root.SetArgs(args)
128		err := root.Execute()
129		return out.String(), errBuf.String(), err
130	}

235		}

20	// MaxOpPayloadBytes caps the decoded byte length of any single op
21	// payload. The cap matches the parent plan's 8 MiB default. Adapters
22	// can negotiate higher caps via capabilities in PR 2's adapter.yaml,
23	// but the type-level constant is the static ceiling and the wire
24	// floor — a hostile peer cannot slip a larger frame through via base64
25	// expansion because the cap is applied to *decoded* bytes.
26	const MaxOpPayloadBytes = 8 * 1024 * 1024
27	
28	// CapabilityLevel is the supported / partial / unsupported tri-state
29	// adapters declare per concept kind.
30	type CapabilityLevel string
31

33		CapabilitySupported   CapabilityLevel = "supported"
34		CapabilityPartial     CapabilityLevel = "partial"
35		CapabilityUnsupported CapabilityLevel = "unsupported"
36	)

38	// OutputMode describes how an adapter declares ownership of an output
39	// path. owned-subdir means the adapter owns the entire subtree;
40	// tool-owned-entry means the adapter owns a specific entry inside a
41	// tool-owned file (an MCP server entry, an AGENTS.md section, etc.);
42	// shared-subdir means the adapter shares the directory with the user and
43	// other tools (e.g. .agents/skills) and the engine manages only the
44	// agent-sync-owned leaf entries within it, never the shared parent.
45	type OutputMode string
46	
47	const (
48		OutputModeOwnedSubdir    OutputMode = "owned-subdir"
49		OutputModeToolOwnedEntry OutputMode = "tool-owned-entry"
50		OutputModeSharedSubdir   OutputMode = "shared-subdir"
51	)
52	
53	// ToolOwnedKind names the structural locator scheme for write_tool_owned
54	// ops. Tied to the OutputMode tool-owned-entry mode.
55	type ToolOwnedKind string
56	
57	const (
58		ToolOwnedKindJSONPointer     ToolOwnedKind = "json-pointer"
59		ToolOwnedKindTOMLPath        ToolOwnedKind = "toml-path"
60		ToolOwnedKindMarkdownSection ToolOwnedKind = "markdown-section"
61	)
62	
63	// WarningStatus classifies an OpWarning as degraded (something the
64	// adapter could partially handle) or partial (capability-matrix shortfall
65	// surfaced as data, not as failure).
66	type WarningStatus string
67	
68	const (
69		WarningStatusDegraded WarningStatus = "degraded"
70		WarningStatusPartial  WarningStatus = "partial"
71	)
72	
73	// Encoding is the wire-only encoding of an op payload.
74	// The Go API never exposes this directly — NewOpWriteFile picks the
75	// encoding automatically based on whether the content is valid UTF-8.
76	type Encoding string
77	
78	const (
79		EncodingUTF8   Encoding = "utf8"
80		EncodingBase64 Encoding = "base64"
81	)
82	
83	// OpKind names the op-discriminator value carried in every op envelope.
84	// The closed v1 set is intentionally small; symlink and any future ops
85	// land in 8b under capability negotiation.
86	type OpKind string
87	
88	const (
89		OpKindWriteFile      OpKind = "write_file"
90		OpKindWriteToolOwned OpKind = "write_tool_owned"
91		OpKindMkdir          OpKind = "mkdir"
92		OpKindDelete         OpKind = "delete"
93		OpKindWarning        OpKind = "warning"
94	)
95	
96	// AllOpKinds returns the closed v1 set in stable order. Mirrors
97	// internal/ir.AllKinds() — useful for capability iteration and
98	// schema parity tests.
99	func AllOpKinds() []OpKind {
100		return []OpKind{
101			OpKindWriteFile,
102			OpKindWriteToolOwned,
103			OpKindMkdir,
104			OpKindDelete,
105			OpKindWarning,
106		}
107	}
108	
109	// Op-level sentinel errors. Callers branch with errors.Is.
110	var (
111		// ErrPayloadTooLarge is returned by NewOpWriteFile when the content
112		// exceeds MaxOpPayloadBytes, and by DecodeOp when a base64-encoded
113		// payload's decoded length exceeds the cap.
114		ErrPayloadTooLarge = errors.New("contract: op payload exceeds MaxOpPayloadBytes")
115	
116		// ErrUnknownOp is returned by DecodeOp when the wire op kind is not
117		// in the v1 closed set, or the "op" discriminator is missing.
118		ErrUnknownOp = errors.New("contract: unknown or missing op kind")
119	
120		// ErrMissingEncoding is returned when a content-bearing op
121		// (write_file, write_tool_owned) is decoded without an "encoding"
122		// field. The wire schemas mark encoding as required; accepting
123		// omission would let schema-invalid ops pass Go-side decoding.
124		ErrMissingEncoding = errors.New("contract: op missing required encoding field")
125	)
126	
127	// InitializeParams is the params object on the initialize request.
128	type InitializeParams struct {
129		Client           string   `json:"client"`
130		ProtocolVersions []string `json:"protocol_versions"`
131		Cookie           string   `json:"cookie"`
132		WorkspaceRoot    string   `json:"workspace_root"`
133		ReservedPrefix   string   `json:"reserved_prefix"`
134		IRVersion        string   `json:"ir_version"`
135		// Scope is the hierarchy level this emit targets: "user", "project",
136		// or "directory". Additive/optional under "freeze the frame, grow
137		// capabilities"; absent or unknown ⇒ treated as "project". Mirrors
138		// adapterkit.InitializeParams.Scope and the initialize.json schema.
139		Scope string          `json:"scope,omitempty"`
140		Meta  json.RawMessage `json:"_meta,omitempty"`
141	}
142	
143	// InitializeResult is the result object the adapter returns from
144	// initialize.
145	//
146	// ProtocolVersion is the version the adapter is committing to speak for
147	// this session. It must be one of the strings the CLI offered in
148	// InitializeParams.ProtocolVersions; the runtime in PR 2 enforces this
149	// and returns ErrorClassAdapterProtocolMismatch on mismatch.
150	type InitializeResult struct {
151		Server          string           `json:"server"`
152		ProtocolVersion string           `json:"protocol_version"`
153		Capabilities    Capabilities     `json:"capabilities"`
154		DeclaredOutputs []DeclaredOutput `json:"declared_outputs"`
155		// Echoed magic cookie value; runtime validates against the per-spawn
156		// AGENT_SYNC_ADAPTER_COOKIE.
157		Cookie string          `json:"cookie,omitempty"`
158		Meta   json.RawMessage `json:"_meta,omitempty"`
159	}
160	
161	// Capabilities is the additive extension point for v1. New fields land
162	// here without bumping the protocol version. The parent plan's "freeze
163	// the wire frame, grow capabilities" rule is the load-bearing invariant.
164	type Capabilities struct {
165		ConceptKinds   map[string]CapabilityLevel `json:"concept_kinds"`
166		WriteToolOwned bool                       `json:"write_tool_owned"`
167		Progress       bool                       `json:"progress"`
168		Meta           json.RawMessage            `json:"_meta,omitempty"`
169	}
170	
171	// DeclaredOutput names a path the adapter intends to write. The CLI
172	// runtime in PR 2 rejects any emit op that names a path outside the
173	// declared set (Bazel declare_file pattern).
174	//
175	// JSONPointer holds an RFC 6901 JSON Pointer (e.g., "/mcpServers/echo")
176	// when Mode is OutputModeToolOwnedEntry and the tool-owned file is
177	// JSON-shaped. Aligns with ToolOwnedKindJSONPointer on OpWriteToolOwned
178	// — both fields use the same locator standard.
179	type DeclaredOutput struct {
180		Path        string          `json:"path"`
181		Mode        OutputMode      `json:"mode"`
182		JSONPointer *string         `json:"json_pointer,omitempty"`
183		SectionID   *string         `json:"section_id,omitempty"`
184		Meta        json.RawMessage `json:"_meta,omitempty"`
185	}
186	
187	// EmitParams is the params object on the emit request. The IR is held
188	// as json.RawMessage to avoid coupling this package up to internal/ir
189	// — PR 2's runtime is responsible for the typed conversion.
190	type EmitParams struct {
191		Target string          `json:"target"`
192		IR     json.RawMessage `json:"ir"`
193		Meta   json.RawMessage `json:"_meta,omitempty"`
